authors_text: Gabriel Wang,Anish Thite,Rodd Talebi,Anthony D'Achille,Alex Mussa,Jason Zutty
title: Evolving SimGANs to Improve Abnormal Electrocardiogram Classification
title_zh: 改进 SimGAN 以改进异常心电图分类
comment: 8 pages, presented at The Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference 2022
comments: 8 pages, presented at The Genetic and Evolutionary Computation Conference 2022
abstract: Machine Learning models are used in a wide variety of domains. However, machine learning methods often require a large amount of data in order to be successful. This is especially troublesome in domains where collecting real-world data is difficult and/or expensive. Data simulators do exist for many of these domains, but they do not sufficiently reflect the real world data due to factors such as a lack of real-world noise. Recently generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been modified to refine simulated image data into data that better fits the real world distribution, using the SimGAN method. While evolutionary computing has been used for GAN evolution, there are currently no frameworks that can evolve a SimGAN. In this paper we (1) extend the SimGAN method to refine one-dimensional data, (2) modify Easy Cartesian Genetic Programming (ezCGP), an evolutionary computing framework, to create SimGANs that more accurately refine simulated data, and (3) create new feature-based quantitative metrics to evaluate refined data. We also use our framework to augment an electrocardiogram (ECG) dataset, a domain that suffers from the issues previously mentioned. In particular, while healthy ECGs can be simulated there are no current simulators of abnormal ECGs. We show that by using an evolved SimGAN to refine simulated healthy ECG data to mimic real-world abnormal ECGs, we can improve the accuracy of abnormal ECG classifiers.
summary: Machine Learning models are used in a wide variety of domains. However, machine learning methods often require a large amount of data in order to be successful. This is especially troublesome in domains where collecting real-world data is difficult and/or expensive. Data simulators do exist for many of these domains, but they do not sufficiently reflect the real world data due to factors such as a lack of real-world noise. Recently generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been modified to refine simulated image data into data that better fits the real world distribution, using the SimGAN method. While evolutionary computing has been used for GAN evolution, there are currently no frameworks that can evolve a SimGAN. In this paper we (1) extend the SimGAN method to refine one-dimensional data, (2) modify Easy Cartesian Genetic Programming (ezCGP), an evolutionary computing framework, to create SimGANs that more accurately refine simulated data, and (3) create new feature-based quantitative metrics to evaluate refined data. We also use our framework to augment an electrocardiogram (ECG) dataset, a domain that suffers from the issues previously mentioned. In particular, while healthy ECGs can be simulated there are no current simulators of abnormal ECGs. We show that by using an evolved SimGAN to refine simulated healthy ECG data to mimic real-world abnormal ECGs, we can improve the accuracy of abnormal ECG classifiers.
abstract_zh: 机器学习模型广泛应用于各个领域。然而，机器学习方法通​​常需要大量数据才能成功。在收集现实世界数据困难和/或昂贵的领域中，这尤其麻烦。其中许多领域确实存在数据模拟器，但由于缺乏现实世界噪声等因素，它们并不能充分反映现实世界的数据。最近，生成对抗网络 (GAN) 已被修改，可以使用 SimGAN 方法将模拟图像数据细化为更适合现实世界分布的数据。虽然进化计算已用于 GAN 进化，但目前还没有可以进化 SimGAN 的框架。在本文中，我们（1）扩展 SimGAN 方法来细化一维数据，（2）修改简单笛卡尔遗传编程（ezCGP）（一种进化计算框架），以创建能够更准确地细化模拟数据的 SimGAN，以及（3）创建新的基于特征的定量指标来评估细化数据。我们还使用我们的框架来增强心电图 (ECG) 数据集，这是一个存在前面提到的问题的领域。特别是，虽然可以模拟健康的心电图，但目前还没有异常心电图的模拟器。我们证明，通过使用进化的 SimGAN 来细化模拟的健康心电图数据以模仿现实世界的异常心电图，我们可以提高异常心电图分类器的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过进化SimGAN方法改进异常心电图（ECG）分类，解决真实异常ECG数据稀缺的问题。
AI.motivation: 真实世界中异常ECG数据难以获取且成本高昂，现有模拟器无法生成足够逼真的异常ECG数据，导致分类模型性能受限。
AI.method: 扩展SimGAN以处理一维数据，改进ezCGP进化框架优化SimGAN参数，并设计基于特征的量化指标评估生成数据质量。
AI.result: 通过进化SimGAN将模拟健康ECG数据转化为类似真实异常ECG的数据，显著提升了异常ECG分类器的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为数据稀缺领域提供了有效解决方案，通过生成更贴近真实分布的数据提升机器学习模型性能。

search_text: Evolving SimGANs to Improve Abnormal Electrocardiogram Classification 改进 SimGAN 以改进异常心电图分类 Machine Learning models are used in a wide variety of domains. However, machine learning methods often require a large amount of data in order to be successful. This is especially troublesome in domains where collecting real-world data is difficult and/or expensive. Data simulators do exist for many of these domains, but they do not sufficiently reflect the real world data due to factors such as a lack of real-world noise. Recently generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been modified to refine simulated image data into data that better fits the real world distribution, using the SimGAN method. While evolutionary computing has been used for GAN evolution, there are currently no frameworks that can evolve a SimGAN. In this paper we (1) extend the SimGAN method to refine one-dimensional data, (2) modify Easy Cartesian Genetic Programming (ezCGP), an evolutionary computing framework, to create SimGANs that more accurately refine simulated data, and (3) create new feature-based quantitative metrics to evaluate refined data. We also use our framework to augment an electrocardiogram (ECG) dataset, a domain that suffers from the issues previously mentioned. In particular, while healthy ECGs can be simulated there are no current simulators of abnormal ECGs. We show that by using an evolved SimGAN to refine simulated healthy ECG data to mimic real-world abnormal ECGs, we can improve the accuracy of abnormal ECG classifiers. 机器学习模型广泛应用于各个领域。然而，机器学习方法通​​常需要大量数据才能成功。在收集现实世界数据困难和/或昂贵的领域中，这尤其麻烦。其中许多领域确实存在数据模拟器，但由于缺乏现实世界噪声等因素，它们并不能充分反映现实世界的数据。最近，生成对抗网络 (GAN) 已被修改，可以使用 SimGAN 方法将模拟图像数据细化为更适合现实世界分布的数据。虽然进化计算已用于 GAN 进化，但目前还没有可以进化 SimGAN 的框架。在本文中，我们（1）扩展 SimGAN 方法来细化一维数据，（2）修改简单笛卡尔遗传编程（ezCGP）（一种进化计算框架），以创建能够更准确地细化模拟数据的 SimGAN，以及（3）创建新的基于特征的定量指标来评估细化数据。我们还使用我们的框架来增强心电图 (ECG) 数据集，这是一个存在前面提到的问题的领域。特别是，虽然可以模拟健康的心电图，但目前还没有异常心电图的模拟器。我们证明，通过使用进化的 SimGAN 来细化模拟的健康心电图数据以模仿现实世界的异常心电图，我们可以提高异常心电图分类器的准确性。 本文提出通过进化SimGAN方法改进异常心电图（ECG）分类，解决真实异常ECG数据稀缺的问题。 Gabriel Wang,Anish Thite,Rodd Talebi,Anthony D'Achille,Alex Mussa,Jason Zutty cs.NE,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Rubén Izquierdo Gonzalo,Carlota Salinas Maldonado,Javier Alonso Ruiz,Ignacio Parra Alonso,David Fernández Llorca,Miguel Á. Sotelo
title: Testing predictive automated driving systems: lessons learned and future recommendations
title_zh: 测试预测性自动驾驶系统：经验教训和未来建议
comment: This work has been accepted to the IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Magazine for publication
comments: This work has been accepted to the IEEE Intelligent Transportation Systems Magazine for publication
abstract: Conventional vehicles are certified through classical approaches, where different physical certification tests are set up on test tracks to assess required safety levels. These approaches are well suited for vehicles with limited complexity and limited interactions with other entities as last-second resources. However, these approaches do not allow to evaluate safety with real behaviors for critical and edge cases, nor to evaluate the ability to anticipate them in the mid or long term. This is particularly relevant for automated and autonomous driving functions that make use of advanced predictive systems to anticipate future actions and motions to be considered in the path planning layer. In this paper, we present and analyze the results of physical tests on proving grounds of several predictive systems in automated driving functions developed within the framework of the BRAVE project. Based on our experience in testing predictive automated driving functions, we identify the main limitations of current physical testing approaches when dealing with predictive systems, analyze the main challenges ahead, and provide a set of practical actions and recommendations to consider in future physical testing procedures for automated and autonomous driving functions.

In this paper, we present and analyze the results of physical tests on proving grounds of several predictive systems in automated driving functions developed within the framework of the BRAVE project. Based on our experience in testing predictive automated driving functions, we identify the main limitations of current physical testing approaches when dealing with predictive systems, analyze the main challenges ahead, and provide a set of practical actions and recommendations to consider in future physical testing procedures for automated and autonomous driving functions.
abstract_zh: 传统车辆通过经典方法进行认证，在测试跑道上设置不同的物理认证测试以评估所需的安全水平。这些方法非常适合复杂性有限且与其他实体交互有限的车辆作为最后一秒的资源。然而，这些方法不允许通过关键和边缘情况的真实行为来评估安全性，也不允许评估在中长期内预测它们的能力。这对于自动驾驶和自动驾驶功能尤其重要，这些功能利用先进的预测系统来预测路径规划层中要考虑的未来行动和动作。在本文中，我们介绍并分析了在 BRAVE 项目框架内开发的自动驾驶功能的几个预测系统的试验场的物理测试结果。根据我们在测试预测性自动驾驶功能方面的经验，我们确定了当前物理测试方法在处理预测系统时的主要局限性，分析了未来的主要挑战，并提供了一系列实际行动和建议，以供在未来的自动化和自主驾驶功能物理测试程序中考虑。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了传统物理测试方法在评估预测式自动驾驶系统时的局限性，并提出未来测试的改进方向。
AI.motivation: 传统车辆认证方法难以有效评估自动驾驶预测系统的长期行为预判能力和复杂场景下的安全性。
AI.method: 基于BRAVE项目，在测试场地对多种预测式自动驾驶系统进行物理测试并分析结果。
AI.result: 发现当前物理测试无法充分验证预测系统的中期/长期行为预判能力，且存在测试场景覆盖不足的问题。
AI.conclusion: 建议未来测试需结合虚拟仿真与物理测试，增加对预测系统动态行为和边缘场景的评估维度。

authors_text: Baihan Lin
title: Evolutionary Multi-Armed Bandits with Genetic Thompson Sampling
title_zh: 采用遗传汤普森采样的进化多臂强盗
comment: Proceeding of IEEE CEC 2022. This work is one of the first works to solve the online learning problems with distributed evolutionary optimizations, and extends our prior work on contextual bandits (e.g. arXiv:2106.15808) by testing against similar simulated and real-world scenarios. Codes at https://github.com/doerlbh/BanditZoo
comments: Proceeding of IEEE CEC 2022. This work is one of the first works to solve the online learning problems with distributed evolutionary optimizations, and extends our prior work on contextual bandits (e.g. arXiv:2106.15808) by testing against similar simulated and real-world scenarios. Codes at https://github.com/doerlbh/BanditZoo
abstract: As two popular schools of machine learning, online learning and evolutionary computations have become two important driving forces behind real-world decision making engines for applications in biomedicine, economics, and engineering fields. Although there are prior work that utilizes bandits to improve evolutionary algorithms' optimization process, it remains a field of blank on how evolutionary approach can help improve the sequential decision making tasks of online learning agents such as the multi-armed bandits. In this work, we propose the Genetic Thompson Sampling, a bandit algorithm that keeps a population of agents and update them with genetic principles such as elite selection, crossover and mutations. Empirical results in multi-armed bandit simulation environments and a practical epidemic control problem suggest that by incorporating the genetic algorithm into the bandit algorithm, our method significantly outperforms the baselines in nonstationary settings. Lastly, we introduce EvoBandit, a web-based interactive visualization to guide the readers through the entire learning process and perform lightweight evaluations on the fly. We hope to engage researchers into this growing field of research with this investigation.
summary: As two popular schools of machine learning, online learning and evolutionary computations have become two important driving forces behind real-world decision making engines for applications in biomedicine, economics, and engineering fields. Although there are prior work that utilizes bandits to improve evolutionary algorithms' optimization process, it remains a field of blank on how evolutionary approach can help improve the sequential decision making tasks of online learning agents such as the multi-armed bandits. In this work, we propose the Genetic Thompson Sampling, a bandit algorithm that keeps a population of agents and update them with genetic principles such as elite selection, crossover and mutations. Empirical results in multi-armed bandit simulation environments and a practical epidemic control problem suggest that by incorporating the genetic algorithm into the bandit algorithm, our method significantly outperforms the baselines in nonstationary settings. Lastly, we introduce EvoBandit, a web-based interactive visualization to guide the readers through the entire learning process and perform lightweight evaluations on the fly. We hope to engage researchers into this growing field of research with this investigation.
abstract_zh: 作为机器学习的两个流行流派，在线学习和进化计算已成为生物医学、经济学和工程领域应用的现实世界决策引擎背后的两个重要驱动力。尽管之前有利用老虎机改进进化算法优化过程的工作，但关于进化方法如何帮助改进在线学习代理（例如多臂老虎机）的顺序决策任务仍然是一个空白。在这项工作中，我们提出了遗传汤普森采样（Genetic Thompson Sampling），这是一种强盗算法，可以保留一组代理并使用精英选择、交叉和突变等遗传原理对其进行更新。多臂强盗模拟环境和实际流行病控制问题的经验结果表明，通过将遗传算法合并到强盗算法中，我们的方法显着优于非平稳设置中的基线。最后，我们介绍 EvoBandit，一种基于网络的交互式可视化工具，可指导读者完成整个学习过程并即时执行轻量级评估。我们希望通过这项调查让研究人员参与到这个不断发展的研究领域。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合遗传算法的汤普森采样方法（Genetic Thompson Sampling），用于解决非平稳环境下的多臂老虎机问题，并开发了交互式可视化工具EvoBandit。
AI.motivation: 尽管已有研究利用老虎机改进进化算法，但如何通过进化方法提升在线学习代理的序列决策能力（如多臂老虎机）仍是空白领域。
AI.method: 设计遗传汤普森采样算法，通过精英选择、交叉和突变等遗传机制维护代理种群，并开发EvoBandit工具实现学习过程可视化与轻量评估。
AI.result: 在多臂老虎机仿真环境和疫情控制实际问题中，该方法在非平稳场景下显著优于基线算法。
AI.conclusion: 遗传机制与老虎机算法的结合有效提升了非平稳环境下的决策性能，EvoBandit工具为该领域研究提供了可视化支持。

Empirical results in multi-armed bandit simulation environments and a practical epidemic control problem suggest that by incorporating the genetic algorithm into the bandit algorithm, our method significantly outperforms the baselines in nonstationary settings. Lastly, we introduce EvoBandit, a web-based interactive visualization to guide the readers through the entire learning process and perform lightweight evaluations on the fly. We hope to engage researchers into this growing field of research with this investigation. 作为机器学习的两个流行流派，在线学习和进化计算已成为生物医学、经济学和工程领域应用的现实世界决策引擎背后的两个重要驱动力。尽管之前有利用老虎机改进进化算法优化过程的工作，但关于进化方法如何帮助改进在线学习代理（例如多臂老虎机）的顺序决策任务仍然是一个空白。在这项工作中，我们提出了遗传汤普森采样（Genetic Thompson Sampling），这是一种强盗算法，可以保留一组代理并使用精英选择、交叉和突变等遗传原理对其进行更新。多臂强盗模拟环境和实际流行病控制问题的经验结果表明，通过将遗传算法合并到强盗算法中，我们的方法显着优于非平稳设置中的基线。最后，我们介绍 EvoBandit，一种基于网络的交互式可视化工具，可指导读者完成整个学习过程并即时执行轻量级评估。我们希望通过这项调查让研究人员参与到这个不断发展的研究领域。 本文提出了一种结合遗传算法的汤普森采样方法（Genetic Thompson Sampling），用于解决非平稳环境下的多臂老虎机问题，并开发了交互式可视化工具EvoBandit。 Baihan Lin cs.NE,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.MA,stat.ML

authors_text: David Ireland,Giovanni Montana
title: LeNSE: Learning To Navigate Subgraph Embeddings for Large-Scale Combinatorial Optimisation
title_zh: LeNSE：学习导航子图嵌入以进行大规模组合优化
abstract: Combinatorial Optimisation problems arise in several application domains and are often formulated in terms of graphs. Many of these problems are NP-hard, but exact solutions are not always needed. Several heuristics have been developed to provide near-optimal solutions; however, they do not typically scale well with the size of the graph. We propose a low-complexity approach for identifying a (possibly much smaller) subgraph of the original graph where the heuristics can be run in reasonable time and with a high likelihood of finding a global near-optimal solution. The core component of our approach is LeNSE, a reinforcement learning algorithm that learns how to navigate the space of possible subgraphs using an Euclidean subgraph embedding as its map. To solve CO problems, LeNSE is provided with a discriminative embedding trained using any existing heuristics using only on a small portion of the original graph. When tested on three problems (vertex cover, max-cut and influence maximisation) using real graphs with up to $10$ million edges, LeNSE identifies small subgraphs yielding solutions comparable to those found by running the heuristics on the entire graph, but at a fraction of the total run time.
summary: Combinatorial Optimisation problems arise in several application domains and are often formulated in terms of graphs. Many of these problems are NP-hard, but exact solutions are not always needed. Several heuristics have been developed to provide near-optimal solutions; however, they do not typically scale well with the size of the graph. We propose a low-complexity approach for identifying a (possibly much smaller) subgraph of the original graph where the heuristics can be run in reasonable time and with a high likelihood of finding a global near-optimal solution. The core component of our approach is LeNSE, a reinforcement learning algorithm that learns how to navigate the space of possible subgraphs using an Euclidean subgraph embedding as its map. To solve CO problems, LeNSE is provided with a discriminative embedding trained using any existing heuristics using only on a small portion of the original graph. When tested on three problems (vertex cover, max-cut and influence maximisation) using real graphs with up to $10$ million edges, LeNSE identifies small subgraphs yielding solutions comparable to those found by running the heuristics on the entire graph, but at a fraction of the total run time.
abstract_zh: 组合优化问题出现在多个应用领域中，并且通常用图来表示。其中许多问题都是 NP 难题，但并不总是需要精确的解决方案。已经开发了几种启发式方法来提供接近最优的解决方案；然而，它们通常不能很好地随着图表的大小而缩放。我们提出了一种低复杂度的方法来识别原始图的（可能小得多）子图，其中启发式可以在合理的时间内运行，并且很有可能找到全局接近最优的解决方案。我们方法的核心组件是 LeNSE，这是一种强化学习算法，它学习如何使用欧几里得子图嵌入作为其映射来导航可能的子图空间。为了解决 CO 问题，LeNSE 提供了一种判别式嵌入，该嵌入使用任何现有的启发式方法进行训练，仅使用原始图的一小部分。当使用具有高达 10 亿美元边的真实图对三个问题（顶点覆盖、最大切割和影响最大化）进行测试时，LeNSE 识别出小子图，产生的解决方案与在整个图上运行启发式算法所找到的解决方案相当，但只花费了总运行时间的一小部分。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于强化学习的子图嵌入导航方法LeNSE，用于解决大规模组合优化问题。
AI.motivation: 传统启发式算法在处理大规模图时计算复杂度高，难以在合理时间内找到近优解。
AI.method: 通过训练区分性子图嵌入模型，利用强化学习算法LeNSE在嵌入空间中导航，定位可运行启发式算法的子图区域。
AI.result: 在顶点覆盖、最大割和影响力最大化三个问题上，LeNSE在包含1000万边的现实图中找到的子图解与全图解质量相当，但运行时间缩短至原时间的分数之一。
AI.conclusion: LeNSE通过子图嵌入导航有效降低了大规模组合优化问题的计算复杂度，保持了解的质量与效率的平衡。

authors_text: Esther Galby,Dániel Marx,Philipp Schepper,Roohani Sharma,Prafullkumar Tale
title: Parameterized Complexity of Weighted Multicut in Trees
title_zh: 树加权多割的参数化复杂度
comment: Full version of the paper accepted for WG 2022
comments: Full version of the paper accepted for WG 2022
abstract: The Edge Multicut problem is a classical cut problem where given an undirected graph $G$, a set of pairs of vertices $\mathcal{P}$, and a budget $k$, the goal is to determine if there is a set $S$ of at most $k$ edges such that for each $(s,t) \in \mathcal{P}$, $G-S$ has no path from $s$ to $t$. Edge Multicut has been relatively recently shown to be fixed-parameter tractable (FPT), parameterized by $k$, by Marx and Razgon [SICOMP 2014], and independently by Bousquet et al. [SICOMP 2018]. In the weighted version of the problem, called Weighted Edge Multicut one is additionally given a weight function $\mathtt{wt} : E(G) \to \mathbb{N}$ and a weight bound $w$, and the goal is to determine if there is a solution of size at most $k$ and weight at most $w$. Both the FPT algorithms for Edge Multicut by Marx et al. and Bousquet et al. fail to generalize to the weighted setting. In fact, the weighted problem is non-trivial even on trees and determining whether Weighted Edge Multicut on trees is FPT was explicitly posed as an open problem by Bousquet et al. [STACS 2009]. In this article, we answer this question positively by designing an algorithm which uses a very recent result by Kim et al. [STOC 2022] about directed flow augmentation as subroutine. We also study a variant of this problem where there is no bound on the size of the solution, but the parameter is a structural property of the input, for example, the number of leaves of the tree. We strengthen our results by stating them for the more general vertex deletion version.

Edge Multicut has been relatively recently shown to be fixed-parameter tractable (FPT), parameterized by $k$, by Marx and Razgon [SICOMP 2014], and independently by Bousquet et al. [SICOMP 2018]. In the weighted version of the problem, called Weighted Edge Multicut one is additionally given a weight function $\mathtt{wt} : E(G) \to \mathbb{N}$ and a weight bound $w$, and the goal is to determine if there is a solution of size at most $k$ and weight at most $w$. Both the FPT algorithms for Edge Multicut by Marx et al. and Bousquet et al. fail to generalize to the weighted setting. In fact, the weighted problem is non-trivial even on trees and determining whether Weighted Edge Multicut on trees is FPT was explicitly posed as an open problem by Bousquet et al. [STACS 2009]. In this article, we answer this question positively by designing an algorithm which uses a very recent result by Kim et al. [STOC 2022] about directed flow augmentation as subroutine. We also study a variant of this problem where there is no bound on the size of the solution, but the parameter is a structural property of the input, for example, the number of leaves of the tree. We strengthen our results by stating them for the more general vertex deletion version.
abstract_zh: 边多割问题是一个经典的割问题，其中给定无向图 $G$、一组顶点对 $\mathcal{P}$ 和预算 $k$，目标是确定是否存在一组最多 $k$ 个边的 $S$，使得对于每个 $(s,t) \in \mathcal{P}$，$G-S$ 没有从 $s$ 到 $t$ 的路径。最近，Marx 和 Razgon [SICOMP 2014] 以及 Bousquet 等人独立地证明了 Edge Multicut 是固定参数易处理的 (FPT)，由 $k$ 参数化。 [SICOMP 2018]。在该问题的加权版本中，称为加权边多切，另外给出一个权重函数 $\mathtt{wt} : E(G) \to \mathbb{N}$ 和一个权重界限 $w$，目标是确定是否存在尺寸至多 $k$ 和权重至多 $w$ 的解决方案。 Marx 等人的 Edge Multicut 的 FPT 算法。和布斯奎特等人。无法推广到加权设置。事实上，即使在树上，加权问题也很重要，确定树上的加权边多重切割是否是 FPT 被 Bousquet 等人明确提出为开放问题。 [STACS 2009]。在本文中，我们通过设计一种算法来积极回答这个问题，该算法使用 Kim 等人的最新结果。 [STOC 2022] 关于作为子程序的定向流量增强。我们还研究了这个问题的一个变体，其中解决方案的大小没有限制，但参数是输入的结构属性，例如树的叶子数量。我们通过为更通用的顶点删除版本陈述它们来加强我们的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了树结构上的加权边多割问题的参数化复杂性，提出了一种基于最新流增强技术的FPT算法。
AI.motivation: 现有FPT算法无法处理加权边多割问题，且该问题在树结构上的参数化复杂性长期未被解决，被列为公开问题。
AI.method: 利用Kim等人关于有向流增强的最新结果作为子程序，设计针对树结构的加权边多割问题的FPT算法，并扩展到顶点删除版本。
AI.result: 证明了树结构上的加权边多割问题属于FPT，并给出了基于叶子数量等结构参数的参数化结果。
AI.conclusion: 成功解决树结构加权边多割的FPT开放问题，为更一般的顶点删除问题提供了理论支持。

authors_text: Yuanhao Cai,Jing Lin,Haoqian Wang,Xin Yuan,Henghui Ding,Yulun Zhang,Radu Timofte,Luc Van Gool
title: Degradation-Aware Unfolding Half-Shuffle Transformer for Spectral Compressive Imaging
title_zh: 用于光谱压缩成像的退化感知展开式半洗牌变压器
comment: NeurIPS 2022; The first Transformer-based deep unfolding method for spectral compressive imaging
comments: NeurIPS 2022; The first Transformer-based deep unfolding method for spectral compressive imaging
abstract: In coded aperture snapshot spectral compressive imaging (CASSI) systems, hyperspectral image (HSI) reconstruction methods are employed to recover the spatial-spectral signal from a compressed measurement. Among these algorithms, deep unfolding methods demonstrate promising performance but suffer from two issues. Firstly, they do not estimate the degradation patterns and ill-posedness degree from the highly related CASSI to guide the iterative learning. Secondly, they are mainly CNN-based, showing limitations in capturing long-range dependencies. In this paper, we propose a principled Degradation-Aware Unfolding Framework (DAUF) that estimates parameters from the compressed image and physical mask, and then uses these parameters to control each iteration. Moreover, we customize a novel Half-Shuffle Transformer (HST) that simultaneously captures local contents and non-local dependencies. By plugging HST into DAUF, we establish the first Transformer-based deep unfolding method, Degradation-Aware Unfolding Half-Shuffle Transformer (DAUHST), for HSI reconstruction. Experiments show that DAUHST significantly surpasses state-of-the-art methods while requiring cheaper computational and memory costs. Code and models will be released at https://github.com/caiyuanhao1998/MST
summary: In coded aperture snapshot spectral compressive imaging (CASSI) systems, hyperspectral image (HSI) reconstruction methods are employed to recover the spatial-spectral signal from a compressed measurement. Among these algorithms, deep unfolding methods demonstrate promising performance but suffer from two issues. Firstly, they do not estimate the degradation patterns and ill-posedness degree from the highly related CASSI to guide the iterative learning. Secondly, they are mainly CNN-based, showing limitations in capturing long-range dependencies. In this paper, we propose a principled Degradation-Aware Unfolding Framework (DAUF) that estimates parameters from the compressed image and physical mask, and then uses these parameters to control each iteration. Moreover, we customize a novel Half-Shuffle Transformer (HST) that simultaneously captures local contents and non-local dependencies. By plugging HST into DAUF, we establish the first Transformer-based deep unfolding method, Degradation-Aware Unfolding Half-Shuffle Transformer (DAUHST), for HSI reconstruction. Experiments show that DAUHST significantly surpasses state-of-the-art methods while requiring cheaper computational and memory costs. Code and models will be released at https://github.com/caiyuanhao1998/MST
abstract_zh: 在编码孔径快照光谱压缩成像（CASSI）系统中，采用高光谱图像（HSI）重建方法从压缩测量中恢复空间光谱信号。在这些算法中，深度展开方法表现出了良好的性能，但存在两个问题。首先，他们没有从高度相关的CASSI中估计退化模式和不适定程度来指导迭代学习。其次，它们主要基于 CNN，在捕获远程依赖关系方面表现出局限性。在本文中，我们提出了一种有原则的退化感知展开框架（DAUF），该框架从压缩图像和物理掩模中估计参数，然后使用这些参数来控制每次迭代。此外，我们定制了一种新颖的 Half-Shuffle Transformer (HST)，它可以同时捕获本地内容和非本地依赖项。通过将 HST 插入 DAUF，我们建立了第一个基于 Transformer 的深度展开方法，即 Degradation-Aware Unfolding Half-Shuffle Transformer (DAUHST)，用于 HSI 重建。实验表明，DAUHST 显着超越了最先进的方法，同时需要更便宜的计算和内存成本。代码和模型将发布在https://github.com/caiyuanhao1998/MST

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的深度展开框架，用于高光谱图像的重建。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度展开方法在高光谱图像重建中存在两个问题：无法从压缩测量中估计退化模式和不适定程度，且主要基于CNN，难以捕捉长距离依赖。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种退化感知的展开框架（DAUF），通过压缩图像和物理掩模估计参数，并使用这些参数控制每次迭代。同时，设计了一种半洗牌Transformer（HST）以同时捕捉局部内容和非局部依赖，并将其集成到DAUF中，形成DAUHST方法。
AI.result: 实验表明，DAUHST在计算和内存成本更低的情况下显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: DAUHST在高光谱图像重建任务中表现出色，同时在计算效率上具有优势。

authors_text: Jing Wang,Haotian Fan,Xiaoxia Hou,Yitian Xu,Tao Li,Xuechao Lu,Lean Fu
title: MSTRIQ: No Reference Image Quality Assessment Based on Swin Transformer with Multi-Stage Fusion
title_zh: MSTRIQ：基于 Swin Transformer 多级融合的无参考图像质量评估
abstract: Measuring the perceptual quality of images automatically is an essential task in the area of computer vision, as degradations on image quality can exist in many processes from image acquisition, transmission to enhancing. Many Image Quality Assessment(IQA) algorithms have been designed to tackle this problem. However, it still remains un settled due to the various types of image distortions and the lack of large-scale human-rated datasets. In this paper, we propose a novel algorithm based on the Swin Transformer [31] with fused features from multiple stages, which aggregates information from both local and global features to better predict the quality. To address the issues of small-scale datasets, relative rankings of images have been taken into account together with regression loss to simultaneously optimize the model. Furthermore, effective data augmentation strategies are also used to improve the performance. In comparisons with previous works, experiments are carried out on two standard IQA datasets and a challenge dataset. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of our work. The proposed method outperforms other methods on standard datasets and ranks 2nd in the no-reference track of NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge [53]. It verifies that our method is promising in solving diverse IQA problems and thus can be used to real-word applications.
summary: Measuring the perceptual quality of images automatically is an essential task in the area of computer vision, as degradations on image quality can exist in many processes from image acquisition, transmission to enhancing. Many Image Quality Assessment(IQA) algorithms have been designed to tackle this problem. However, it still remains un settled due to the various types of image distortions and the lack of large-scale human-rated datasets. In this paper, we propose a novel algorithm based on the Swin Transformer [31] with fused features from multiple stages, which aggregates information from both local and global features to better predict the quality. To address the issues of small-scale datasets, relative rankings of images have been taken into account together with regression loss to simultaneously optimize the model. Furthermore, effective data augmentation strategies are also used to improve the performance. In comparisons with previous works, experiments are carried out on two standard IQA datasets and a challenge dataset. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of our work. The proposed method outperforms other methods on standard datasets and ranks 2nd in the no-reference track of NTIRE 2022 Perceptual Image Quality Assessment Challenge [53]. It verifies that our method is promising in solving diverse IQA problems and thus can be used to real-word applications.
abstract_zh: 自动测量图像的感知质量是计算机视觉领域的一项重要任务，因为从图像采集、传输到增强的许多过程中都可能存在图像质量的下降。许多图像质量评估（IQA）算法被设计来解决这个问题。然而，由于各种类型的图像失真和缺乏大规模的人类评级数据集，该问题仍然悬而未决。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 Swin Transformer [31] 的新颖算法，该算法融合了多个阶段的特征，该算法聚合来自局部和全局特征的信息，以更好地预测质量。为了解决小规模数据集的问题，将图像的相对排名与回归损失一起考虑在内，以同时优化模型。此外，还使用有效的数据增强策略来提高性能。与之前的工作相比，在两个标准 IQA 数据集和一个挑战数据集上进行了实验。结果证明了我们工作的有效性。所提出的方法在标准数据集上优于其他方法，并在 NTIRE 2022 感知图像质量评估挑战赛的无参考赛道中排名第二[53]。它验证了我们的方法在解决各种 IQA 问题方面很有前景，因此可以用于实际应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于Swin Transformer的多阶段融合无参考图像质量评估算法MSTRIQ，以解决图像质量退化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有IQA算法难以应对多样化的图像失真类型及缺乏大规模人工评分数据集的问题，需设计更鲁棒的评估方法。
AI.method: 采用Swin Transformer架构，融合多阶段局部与全局特征；结合图像相对排序与回归损失优化模型，并应用有效数据增强策略。
AI.result: 在两个标准IQA数据集和NTIRE 2022挑战数据集上优于现有方法，在无参考赛道中排名第二。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在处理多样化IQA问题上表现优异，具有实际应用价值。

Many Image Quality Assessment(IQA) algorithms have been designed to tackle this problem. authors_text: Takuto Otomo,Hiroshi Kera,Kazuhiko Kawamoto
title: Adversarial joint attacks on legged robots
title_zh: 对有腿机器人的对抗性联合攻击
abstract: We address adversarial attacks on the actuators at the joints of legged robots trained by deep reinforcement learning. The vulnerability to the joint attacks can significantly impact the safety and robustness of legged robots. In this study, we demonstrate that the adversarial perturbations to the torque control signals of the actuators can significantly reduce the rewards and cause walking instability in robots. To find the adversarial torque perturbations, we develop black-box adversarial attacks, where, the adversary cannot access the neural networks trained by deep reinforcement learning. The black box attack can be applied to legged robots regardless of the architecture and algorithms of deep reinforcement learning. We employ three search methods for the black-box adversarial attacks: random search, differential evolution, and numerical gradient descent methods. In experiments with the quadruped robot Ant-v2 and the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2, in OpenAI Gym environments, we find that differential evolution can efficiently find the strongest torque perturbations among the three methods. In addition, we realize that the quadruped robot Ant-v2 is vulnerable to the adversarial perturbations, whereas the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2 is robust to the perturbations. Consequently, the joint attacks can be used for proactive diagnosis of robot walking instability.
summary: We address adversarial attacks on the actuators at the joints of legged robots trained by deep reinforcement learning. The vulnerability to the joint attacks can significantly impact the safety and robustness of legged robots. In this study, we demonstrate that the adversarial perturbations to the torque control signals of the actuators can significantly reduce the rewards and cause walking instability in robots. To find the adversarial torque perturbations, we develop black-box adversarial attacks, where, the adversary cannot access the neural networks trained by deep reinforcement learning. The black box attack can be applied to legged robots regardless of the architecture and algorithms of deep reinforcement learning. We employ three search methods for the black-box adversarial attacks: random search, differential evolution, and numerical gradient descent methods. In experiments with the quadruped robot Ant-v2 and the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2, in OpenAI Gym environments, we find that differential evolution can efficiently find the strongest torque perturbations among the three methods. In addition, we realize that the quadruped robot Ant-v2 is vulnerable to the adversarial perturbations, whereas the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2 is robust to the perturbations. Consequently, the joint attacks can be used for proactive diagnosis of robot walking instability.
abstract_zh: 我们解决了对经过深度强化学习训练的腿式机器人关节执行器的对抗性攻击。联合攻击的脆弱性会严重影响腿式机器人的安全性和鲁棒性。在这项研究中，我们证明对执行器扭矩控制信号的对抗性扰动会显着降低奖励并导致机器人行走不稳定。为了找到对抗性扭矩扰动，我们开发了黑盒对抗性攻击，其中对手无法访问经过深度强化学习训练的神经网络。无论深度强化学习的架构和算法如何，黑盒攻击都可以应用于有腿机器人。我们采用三种搜索方法来进行黑盒对抗攻击：随机搜索、差分进化和数值梯度下降方法。在 OpenAI Gym 环境中四足机器人 Ant-v2 和双足机器人 Humanoid-v2 的实验中，我们发现差分进化可以有效地找到三种方法中最强的扭矩扰动。此外，我们意识到四足机器人 Ant-v2 容易受到对抗性扰动的影响，而双足机器人 Humanoid-v2 对扰动具有鲁棒性。因此，联合攻击可用于机器人行走不稳定性的主动诊断。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了针对通过深度强化学习训练的腿部机器人的关节对抗攻击问题。
AI.motivation: 腿部机器人在安全性和鲁棒性方面容易受到关节攻击的影响，因此需要研究对抗性扰动对其控制信号的影响。
AI.method: 本文开发了黑盒对抗攻击方法，采用随机搜索、差分进化和数值梯度下降三种搜索方法寻找对抗性扭矩扰动。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，差分进化方法在三种方法中效率最高，四足机器人Ant-v2对对抗扰动敏感，而双足机器人Humanoid-v2则相对稳健。
AI.conclusion: 关节攻击可以用于主动诊断机器人行走不稳定问题，揭示了不同机器人架构对对抗扰动的敏感性差异。

ai_content.tldr: 本文研究了针对通过深度强化学习训练的腿部机器人的关节对抗攻击问题。

search_text: Adversarial joint attacks on legged robots 对有腿机器人的对抗性联合攻击 We address adversarial attacks on the actuators at the joints of legged robots trained by deep reinforcement learning. The vulnerability to the joint attacks can significantly impact the safety and robustness of legged robots. In this study, we demonstrate that the adversarial perturbations to the torque control signals of the actuators can significantly reduce the rewards and cause walking instability in robots. To find the adversarial torque perturbations, we develop black-box adversarial attacks, where, the adversary cannot access the neural networks trained by deep reinforcement learning. The black box attack can be applied to legged robots regardless of the architecture and algorithms of deep reinforcement learning. We employ three search methods for the black-box adversarial attacks: random search, differential evolution, and numerical gradient descent methods. In experiments with the quadruped robot Ant-v2 and the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2, in OpenAI Gym environments, we find that differential evolution can efficiently find the strongest torque perturbations among the three methods. In addition, we realize that the quadruped robot Ant-v2 is vulnerable to the adversarial perturbations, whereas the bipedal robot Humanoid-v2 is robust to the perturbations. Consequently, the joint attacks can be used for proactive diagnosis of robot walking instability. 我们解决了对经过深度强化学习训练的腿式机器人关节执行器的对抗性攻击。联合攻击的脆弱性会严重影响腿式机器人的安全性和鲁棒性。在这项研究中，我们证明对执行器扭矩控制信号的对抗性扰动会显着降低奖励并导致机器人行走不稳定。为了找到对抗性扭矩扰动，我们开发了黑盒对抗性攻击，其中对手无法访问经过深度强化学习训练的神经网络。无论深度强化学习的架构和算法如何，黑盒攻击都可以应用于有腿机器人。我们采用三种搜索方法来进行黑盒对抗攻击：随机搜索、差分进化和数值梯度下降方法。在 OpenAI Gym 环境中四足机器人 Ant-v2 和双足机器人 Humanoid-v2 的实验中，我们发现差分进化可以有效地找到三种方法中最强的扭矩扰动。此外，我们意识到四足机器人 Ant-v2 容易受到对抗性扰动的影响，而双足机器人 Humanoid-v2 对扰动具有鲁棒性。因此，联合攻击可用于机器人行走不稳定性的主动诊断。 本文研究了针对通过深度强化学习训练的腿部机器人的关节对抗攻击问题。 Takuto Otomo,Hiroshi Kera,Kazuhiko Kawamoto cs.RO,cs.AI

authors_text: Tao Yang,Yuwang Wang,Yan Lu,Nanning Zheng
title: Visual Concepts Tokenization
title_zh: 视觉概念标记化
abstract: Obtaining the human-like perception ability of abstracting visual concepts from concrete pixels has always been a fundamental and important target in machine learning research fields such as disentangled representation learning and scene decomposition. Towards this goal, we propose an unsupervised transformer-based Visual Concepts Tokenization framework, dubbed VCT, to perceive an image into a set of disentangled visual concept tokens, with each concept token responding to one type of independent visual concept. Particularly, to obtain these concept tokens, we only use cross-attention to extract visual information from the image tokens layer by layer without self-attention between concept tokens, preventing information leakage across concept tokens. We further propose a Concept Disentangling Loss to facilitate that different concept tokens represent independent visual concepts. The cross-attention and disentangling loss play the role of induction and mutual exclusion for the concept tokens, respectively. Extensive experiments on several popular datasets verify the effectiveness of VCT on the tasks of disentangled representation learning and scene decomposition. VCT achieves the state of the art results by a large margin.
summary: Obtaining the human-like perception ability of abstracting visual concepts from concrete pixels has always been a fundamental and important target in machine learning research fields such as disentangled representation learning and scene decomposition. Towards this goal, we propose an unsupervised transformer-based Visual Concepts Tokenization framework, dubbed VCT, to perceive an image into a set of disentangled visual concept tokens, with each concept token responding to one type of independent visual concept. Particularly, to obtain these concept tokens, we only use cross-attention to extract visual information from the image tokens layer by layer without self-attention between concept tokens, preventing information leakage across concept tokens. We further propose a Concept Disentangling Loss to facilitate that different concept tokens represent independent visual concepts. The cross-attention and disentangling loss play the role of induction and mutual exclusion for the concept tokens, respectively. Extensive experiments on several popular datasets verify the effectiveness of VCT on the tasks of disentangled representation learning and scene decomposition. VCT achieves the state of the art results by a large margin.
abstract_zh: 获得从具体像素中抽象视觉概念的类似人类的感知能力一直是解纠缠表示学习和场景分解等机器学习研究领域的基础和重要目标。为了实现这一目标，我们提出了一种基于 Transformer 的无监督视觉概念标记化框架，称为 VCT，将图像感知为一组解开的视觉概念标记，每个概念标记响应一种独立的视觉概念。特别是，为了获得这些概念标记，我们仅使用交叉注意力从图像标记中逐层提取视觉信息，而不使用概念标记之间的自注意力，从而防止跨概念标记的信息泄漏。我们进一步提出了概念解缠损失，以促进不同的概念标记代表独立的视觉概念。交叉注意力和解缠结损失分别对概念标记起到归纳和互斥的作用。对几个流行数据集的大量实验验证了 VCT 在解缠结表示学习和场景分解任务上的有效性。 VCT 大幅实现了最先进的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无监督的基于Transformer的视觉概念分词框架VCT，将图像分解为独立的视觉概念标记。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在解耦表示学习和场景分解任务中难以有效提取独立视觉概念，需探索更高效的无监督学习框架。
AI.method: 通过交叉注意力逐层提取图像特征，避免概念标记间的自注意力以防止信息泄露，并引入概念解耦损失促进标记独立性。
AI.result: 在多个主流数据集上验证了VCT在解耦表示学习和场景分解任务中的有效性，达到当前最优结果。
AI.conclusion: VCT框架通过交叉注意力与解耦损失的协同作用，成功实现了视觉概念的独立表征，为相关研究提供了新思路。

authors_text: Jayabrata Chowdhury,Suresh Sundaram,Nishanth Rao,Narasimhan Sundararajan
title: An efficient Deep Spatio-Temporal Context Aware decision Network (DST-CAN) for Predictive Manoeuvre Planning
title_zh: 用于预测机动规划的高效深度时空上下文感知决策网络 (DST-CAN)
abstract: To ensure the safety and efficiency of its maneuvers, an Autonomous Vehicle (AV) should anticipate the future intentions of surrounding vehicles using its sensor information. If an AV can predict its surrounding vehicles' future trajectories, it can make safe and efficient manoeuvre decisions. In this paper, we present such a Deep Spatio-Temporal Context-Aware decision Network (DST-CAN) model for predictive manoeuvre planning of AVs. A memory neuron network is used to predict future trajectories of its surrounding vehicles. The driving environment's spatio-temporal information (past, present, and predicted future trajectories) are embedded into a context-aware grid. The proposed DST-CAN model employs these context-aware grids as inputs to a convolutional neural network to understand the spatial relationships between the vehicles and determine a safe and efficient manoeuvre decision. The DST-CAN model also uses information of human driving behavior on a highway. Performance evaluation of DST-CAN has been carried out using two publicly available NGSIM US-101 and I-80 datasets. Also, rule-based ground truth decisions have been compared with those generated by DST-CAN. The results clearly show that DST-CAN can make much better decisions with 3-sec of predicted trajectories of neighboring vehicles compared to currently existing methods that do not use this prediction.
summary: To ensure the safety and efficiency of its maneuvers, an Autonomous Vehicle (AV) should anticipate the future intentions of surrounding vehicles using its sensor information. If an AV can predict its surrounding vehicles' future trajectories, it can make safe and efficient manoeuvre decisions. In this paper, we present such a Deep Spatio-Temporal Context-Aware decision Network (DST-CAN) model for predictive manoeuvre planning of AVs. A memory neuron network is used to predict future trajectories of its surrounding vehicles. The driving environment's spatio-temporal information (past, present, and predicted future trajectories) are embedded into a context-aware grid. The proposed DST-CAN model employs these context-aware grids as inputs to a convolutional neural network to understand the spatial relationships between the vehicles and determine a safe and efficient manoeuvre decision. The DST-CAN model also uses information of human driving behavior on a highway. Performance evaluation of DST-CAN has been carried out using two publicly available NGSIM US-101 and I-80 datasets. Also, rule-based ground truth decisions have been compared with those generated by DST-CAN. The results clearly show that DST-CAN can make much better decisions with 3-sec of predicted trajectories of neighboring vehicles compared to currently existing methods that do not use this prediction.
abstract_zh: 为了确保其操纵的安全性和效率，自动驾驶车辆（AV）应使用其传感器信息来预测周围车辆的未来意图。如果自动驾驶汽车能够预测周围车辆的未来轨迹，它就可以做出安全有效的机动决策。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于自动驾驶汽车预测机动规划的深度时空上下文感知决策网络（DST-CAN）模型。记忆神经元网络用于预测周围车辆的未来轨迹。驾驶环境的时空信息（过去、现在和预测的未来轨迹）被嵌入到上下文感知网格中。所提出的 DST-CAN 模型采用这些上下文感知网格作为卷积神经网络的输入，以了解车辆之间的空间关系并确定安全有效的机动决策。 DST-CAN 模型还使用高速公路上人类驾驶行为的信息。 DST-CAN 的性能评估是使用两个公开可用的 NGSIM US-101 和 I-80 数据集进行的。此外，基于规则的真实决策与 DST-CAN 生成的决策进行了比较。结果清楚地表明，与目前不使用此预测的现有方法相比，DST-CAN 可以通过 3 秒的邻近车辆预测轨迹做出更好的决策。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种深度时空上下文感知决策网络（DST-CAN），用于自动驾驶车辆的预测性驾驶决策，通过预测周围车辆轨迹以提高安全性和效率。
AI.motivation: 现有方法未能有效利用周围车辆未来轨迹预测信息，导致自动驾驶决策的安全性与效率不足，需更精准的预测模型支持。
AI.method: 采用记忆神经网络预测周围车辆轨迹，将时空信息（过去、现在及预测轨迹）嵌入上下文感知网格，通过卷积神经网络分析车辆空间关系并结合高速公路人类驾驶行为数据生成决策。
AI.result: 基于NGSIM US-101和I-80数据集的实验表明，DST-CAN在3秒预测轨迹条件下显著优于现有方法，决策效果更优。
AI.conclusion: DST-CAN通过融合时空上下文信息与人类驾驶行为，有效提升了自动驾驶车辆的预测性决策能力，验证了其在安全与效率上的优势。

method: 采用记忆神经网络预测周围车辆轨迹，将时空信息（过去、现在及预测轨迹）嵌入上下文感知网格，通过卷积神经网络分析车辆空间关系并结合高速公路人类驾驶行为数据生成决策。
result: 基于NGSIM US-101和I-80数据集的实验表明，DST-CAN在3秒预测轨迹条件下显著优于现有方法，决策效果更优。
conclusion: DST-CAN通过融合时空上下文信息与人类驾驶行为，有效提升了自动驾驶车辆的预测性决策能力，验证了其在安全与效率上的优势。
search_text: An efficient Deep Spatio-Temporal Context Aware decision Network (DST-CAN) for Predictive Manoeuvre Planning 用于预测机动规划的高效深度时空上下文感知决策网络 (DST-CAN) To ensure the safety and efficiency of its maneuvers, an Autonomous Vehicle (AV) should anticipate the future intentions of surrounding vehicles using its sensor information. If an AV can predict its surrounding vehicles' future trajectories, it can make safe and efficient manoeuvre decisions. In this paper, we present such a Deep Spatio-Temporal Context-Aware decision Network (DST-CAN) model for predictive manoeuvre planning of AVs. A memory neuron network is used to predict future trajectories of its surrounding vehicles. The driving environment's spatio-temporal information (past, present, and predicted future trajectories) are embedded into a context-aware grid. The proposed DST-CAN model employs these context-aware grids as inputs to a convolutional neural network to understand the spatial relationships between the vehicles and determine a safe and efficient manoeuvre decision. The DST-CAN model also uses information of human driving behavior on a highway. Performance evaluation of DST-CAN has been carried out using two publicly available NGSIM US-101 and I-80 datasets. Also, rule-based ground truth decisions have been compared with those generated by DST-CAN. The results clearly show that DST-CAN can make much better decisions with 3-sec of predicted trajectories of neighboring vehicles compared to currently existing methods that do not use this prediction. 为了确保其操纵的安全性和效率，自动驾驶车辆（AV）应使用其传感器信息来预测周围车辆的未来意图。如果自动驾驶汽车能够预测周围车辆的未来轨迹，它就可以做出安全有效的机动决策。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于自动驾驶汽车预测机动规划的深度时空上下文感知决策网络（DST-CAN）模型。记忆神经元网络用于预测周围车辆的未来轨迹。驾驶环境的时空信息（过去、现在和预测的未来轨迹）被嵌入到上下文感知网格中。所提出的 DST-CAN 模型采用这些上下文感知网格作为卷积神经网络的输入，以了解车辆之间的空间关系并确定安全有效的机动决策。 DST-CAN 模型还使用高速公路上人类驾驶行为的信息。 DST-CAN 的性能评估是使用两个公开可用的 NGSIM US-101 和 I-80 数据集进行的。此外，基于规则的真实决策与 DST-CAN 生成的决策进行了比较。结果清楚地表明，与目前不使用此预测的现有方法相比，DST-CAN 可以通过 3 秒的邻近车辆预测轨迹做出更好的决策。 本文提出一种深度时空上下文感知决策网络（DST-CAN），用于自动驾驶车辆的预测性驾驶决策，通过预测周围车辆轨迹以提高安全性和效率。 Jayabrata Chowdhury,Suresh Sundaram,Nishanth Rao,Narasimhan Sundararajan cs.RO

authors_text: Reza Nasirigerdeh,Reihaneh Torkzadehmahani,Daniel Rueckert,Georgios Kaissis
title: Kernel Normalized Convolutional Networks
title_zh: 核归一化卷积网络
abstract: Existing convolutional neural network architectures frequently rely upon batch normalization (BatchNorm) to effectively train the model. BatchNorm, however, performs poorly with small batch sizes, and is inapplicable to differential privacy. To address these limitations, we propose the kernel normalization (KernelNorm) and kernel normalized convolutional layers, and incorporate them into kernel normalized convolutional networks (KNConvNets) as the main building blocks. We implement KNConvNets corresponding to the state-of-the-art ResNets while forgoing the BatchNorm layers. Through extensive experiments, we illustrate that KNConvNets achieve higher or competitive performance compared to the BatchNorm counterparts in image classification and semantic segmentation. They also significantly outperform their batch-independent competitors including those based on layer and group normalization in non-private and differentially private training. Given that, KernelNorm combines the batch-independence property of layer and group normalization with the performance advantage of BatchNorm.
summary: Existing convolutional neural network architectures frequently rely upon batch normalization (BatchNorm) to effectively train the model. BatchNorm, however, performs poorly with small batch sizes, and is inapplicable to differential privacy. To address these limitations, we propose the kernel normalization (KernelNorm) and kernel normalized convolutional layers, and incorporate them into kernel normalized convolutional networks (KNConvNets) as the main building blocks. We implement KNConvNets corresponding to the state-of-the-art ResNets while forgoing the BatchNorm layers. Through extensive experiments, we illustrate that KNConvNets achieve higher or competitive performance compared to the BatchNorm counterparts in image classification and semantic segmentation. They also significantly outperform their batch-independent competitors including those based on layer and group normalization in non-private and differentially private training. Given that, KernelNorm combines the batch-independence property of layer and group normalization with the performance advantage of BatchNorm.
abstract_zh: 现有的卷积神经网络架构经常依赖批量标准化（BatchNorm）来有效地训练模型。然而，BatchNorm 在小批量时表现不佳，并且不适用于差分隐私。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了内核归一化（KernelNorm）和内核归一化卷积层，并将它们合并到内核归一化卷积网络（KNConvNets）中作为主要构建块。我们实现了与最先进的 ResNet 相对应的 KNConvNet，同时放弃了 BatchNorm 层。通过大量实验，我们证明 KNConvNet 在图像分类和语义分割方面比 BatchNorm 同类产品实现了更高或有竞争力的性能。他们还显着优于批次独立的竞争对手，包括那些基于非私人和差异私人培训中的层和组标准化的竞争对手。鉴于此，KernelNorm 将层和组标准化的批次独立性与 BatchNorm 的性能优势结合起来。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为KernelNorm的归一化方法及其对应的核归一化卷积网络（KNConvNets），以解决传统批量归一化（BatchNorm）在小批量尺寸和差分隐私场景下的局限性。
AI.motivation: 现有卷积神经网络依赖BatchNorm，但其在小批量训练时效果差且无法满足差分隐私需求，而层归一化等替代方法在性能上存在不足。
AI.method: 设计了核归一化（KernelNorm）和核归一化卷积层，构建KNConvNets架构，替代ResNet中的BatchNorm模块，并在图像分类和语义分割任务中进行验证。
AI.result: KNConvNets在非隐私和差分隐私场景下均优于BatchNorm及层归一化、组归一化等方法，且在小批量训练中保持竞争力。
AI.conclusion: KernelNorm结合了层/组归一化的批量独立性与BatchNorm的性能优势，为深度学习提供了更鲁棒的归一化方案。

authors_text: Ratha Siv,Matei Mancas,Bernard Gosselin,Dona Valy,Sokchenda Sreng
title: People Tracking and Re-Identifying in Distributed Contexts: Extension Study of PoseTReID
title_zh: 分布式环境中的人员跟踪和重新识别：PoseTReID 的扩展研究
comment: 6 pages, 5 figures, 3 tables, To be submitted to EECSI2022
comments: 6 pages, 5 figures, 3 tables, To be submitted to EECSI2022
abstract: In our previous paper, we introduced PoseTReID which is a generic framework for real-time 2D multi-person tracking in distributed interaction spaces where long-term people's identities are important for other studies such as behavior analysis, etc. In this paper, we introduce a further study of PoseTReID framework in order to give a more complete comprehension of the framework. We use a well-known bounding box detector YOLO (v4) for the detection to compare to OpenPose which was used in our last paper, and we use SORT and DeepSORT to compare to centroid which was also used previously, and most importantly for the re-identification, we use a bunch of deep leaning methods such as MLFN, OSNet, and OSNet-AIN with our custom classification layer to compare to FaceNet which was also used earlier in our last paper. By evaluating on our PoseTReID datasets, even though those deep learning re-identification methods are designed for only short-term re-identification across multiple cameras or videos, it is worth showing that they give impressive results which boost the overall tracking performance of PoseTReID framework regardless the type of tracking method. At the same time, we also introduce our research-friendly and open source Python toolbox pyppbox, which is purely written in Python and contains all sub-modules which are used in this study along with real-time online and offline evaluations for our PoseTReID datasets. This pyppbox is available on GitHub https://github.com/rathaumons/pyppbox .
summary: In our previous paper, we introduced PoseTReID which is a generic framework for real-time 2D multi-person tracking in distributed interaction spaces where long-term people's identities are important for other studies such as behavior analysis, etc. In this paper, we introduce a further study of PoseTReID framework in order to give a more complete comprehension of the framework. We use a well-known bounding box detector YOLO (v4) for the detection to compare to OpenPose which was used in our last paper, and we use SORT and DeepSORT to compare to centroid which was also used previously, and most importantly for the re-identification, we use a bunch of deep leaning methods such as MLFN, OSNet, and OSNet-AIN with our custom classification layer to compare to FaceNet which was also used earlier in our last paper. By evaluating on our PoseTReID datasets, even though those deep learning re-identification methods are designed for only short-term re-identification across multiple cameras or videos, it is worth showing that they give impressive results which boost the overall tracking performance of PoseTReID framework regardless the type of tracking method. At the same time, we also introduce our research-friendly and open source Python toolbox pyppbox, which is purely written in Python and contains all sub-modules which are used in this study along with real-time online and offline evaluations for our PoseTReID datasets. This pyppbox is available on GitHub https://github.com/rathaumons/pyppbox .
abstract_zh: 在我们之前的论文中，我们介绍了 PoseTReID，它是分布式交互空间中实时 2D 多人跟踪的通用框架，其中长期人员身份对于行为分析等其他研究很重要。在本文中，我们介绍了对 PoseTReID 框架的进一步研究，以便更全面地理解该框架。我们使用著名的边界框检测器 YOLO (v4) 进行检测，与我们上一篇论文中使用的 OpenPose 进行比较，我们使用 SORT 和 DeepSORT 与之前也使用过的质心进行比较，最重要的是，对于重新识别，我们使用了一系列深度学习方法，例如 MLFN、OSNet 和 OSNet-AIN 以及我们的自定义分类层，与我们之前在上一篇论文中使用的 FaceNet 进行比较。通过对我们的 PoseTReID 数据集进行评估，尽管这些深度学习重新识别方法仅设计用于跨多个摄像机或视频的短期重新识别，但值得表明的是，它们给出了令人印象深刻的结果，无论跟踪方法的类型如何，这些结果都提高了 PoseTReID 框架的整体跟踪性能。同时，我们还介绍了我们的研究友好型开源Python工具箱pyppbox，它纯粹用Python编写，包含本研究中使用的所有子模块以及对我们的PoseTReID数据集的实时在线和离线评估。此 pyppbox 可在 GitHub https://github.com/rathaumons/pyppbox 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文对PoseTReID框架进行扩展研究，通过比较不同检测与重识别方法提升分布式场景下多人跟踪性能，并发布开源工具包pyppbox。
AI.motivation: 为更全面理解PoseTReID框架，需验证其在不同检测算法（如YOLOv4替代OpenPose）和重识别模型（如OSNet替代FaceNet）下的表现，同时提供研究工具支持。
AI.method: 采用YOLOv4进行目标检测，对比SORT/DeepSORT跟踪算法；使用MLFN、OSNet-AIN等深度学习模型进行重识别实验，并开发开源Python工具包pyppbox实现实时评估。
AI.result: 深度学习重识别方法显著提升PoseTReID整体跟踪效果，且pyppbox工具包支持在线/离线评估，验证了框架的灵活性与实用性。
AI.conclusion: 通过方法对比验证了PoseTReID框架的有效性，开源工具包为后续研究提供便利，未来可进一步优化跨摄像头长期跟踪性能。

search_text: People Tracking and Re-Identifying in Distributed Contexts: Extension Study of PoseTReID 分布式环境中的人员跟踪和重新识别：PoseTReID 的扩展研究 In our previous paper, we introduced PoseTReID which is a generic framework for real-time 2D multi-person tracking in distributed interaction spaces where long-term people's identities are important for other studies such as behavior analysis, etc. In this paper, we introduce a further study of PoseTReID framework in order to give a more complete comprehension of the framework. We use a well-known bounding box detector YOLO (v4) for the detection to compare to OpenPose which was used in our last paper, and we use SORT and DeepSORT to compare to centroid which was also used previously, and most importantly for the re-identification, we use a bunch of deep leaning methods such as MLFN, OSNet, and OSNet-AIN with our custom classification layer to compare to FaceNet which was also used earlier in our last paper. By evaluating on our PoseTReID datasets, even though those deep learning re-identification methods are designed for only short-term re-identification across multiple cameras or videos, it is worth showing that they give impressive results which boost the overall tracking performance of PoseTReID framework regardless the type of tracking method. At the same time, we also introduce our research-friendly and open source Python toolbox pyppbox, which is purely written in Python and contains all sub-modules which are used in this study along with real-time online and offline evaluations for our PoseTReID datasets. This pyppbox is available on GitHub https://github.com/rathaumons/pyppbox . 在我们之前的论文中，我们介绍了 PoseTReID，它是分布式交互空间中实时 2D 多人跟踪的通用框架，其中长期人员身份对于行为分析等其他研究很重要。在本文中，我们介绍了对 PoseTReID 框架的进一步研究，以便更全面地理解该框架。我们使用著名的边界框检测器 YOLO (v4) 进行检测，与我们上一篇论文中使用的 OpenPose 进行比较，我们使用 SORT 和 DeepSORT 与之前也使用过的质心进行比较，最重要的是，对于重新识别，我们使用了一系列深度学习方法，例如 MLFN、OSNet 和 OSNet-AIN 以及我们的自定义分类层，与我们之前在上一篇论文中使用的 FaceNet 进行比较。通过对我们的 PoseTReID 数据集进行评估，尽管这些深度学习重新识别方法仅设计用于跨多个摄像机或视频的短期重新识别，但值得表明的是，它们给出了令人印象深刻的结果，无论跟踪方法的类型如何，这些结果都提高了 PoseTReID 框架的整体跟踪性能。同时，我们还介绍了我们的研究友好型开源Python工具箱pyppbox，它纯粹用Python编写，包含本研究中使用的所有子模块以及对我们的PoseTReID数据集的实时在线和离线评估。此 pyppbox 可在 GitHub https://github.com/rathaumons/pyppbox 上获取。 本文对PoseTReID框架进行扩展研究，通过比较不同检测与重识别方法提升分布式场景下多人跟踪性能，并发布开源工具包pyppbox。 Ratha Siv,Matei Mancas,Bernard Gosselin,Dona Valy,Sokchenda Sreng cs.CV

authors_text: Ehsan Mokhtarian,Saber Salehkaleybar,AmirEmad Ghassami,Negar Kiyavash
title: A Unified Experiment Design Approach for Cyclic and Acyclic Causal Models
title_zh: 循环和非循环因果模型的统一实验设计方法
comment: 31 pages, 6 figures, 1 table, accepted in JMLR
comments: 31 pages, 6 figures, 1 table, accepted in JMLR
abstract: We study experiment design for unique identification of the causal graph of a simple SCM, where the graph may contain cycles. The presence of cycles in the structure introduces major challenges for experiment design as, unlike acyclic graphs, learning the skeleton of causal graphs with cycles may not be possible from merely the observational distribution. Furthermore, intervening on a variable in such graphs does not necessarily lead to orienting all the edges incident to it. In this paper, we propose an experiment design approach that can learn both cyclic and acyclic graphs and hence, unifies the task of experiment design for both types of graphs. We provide a lower bound on the number of experiments required to guarantee the unique identification of the causal graph in the worst case, showing that the proposed approach is order-optimal in terms of the number of experiments up to an additive logarithmic term. Moreover, we extend our result to the setting where the size of each experiment is bounded by a constant. For this case, we show that our approach is optimal in terms of the size of the largest experiment required for uniquely identifying the causal graph in the worst case.
summary: We study experiment design for unique identification of the causal graph of a simple SCM, where the graph may contain cycles. The presence of cycles in the structure introduces major challenges for experiment design as, unlike acyclic graphs, learning the skeleton of causal graphs with cycles may not be possible from merely the observational distribution. Furthermore, intervening on a variable in such graphs does not necessarily lead to orienting all the edges incident to it. In this paper, we propose an experiment design approach that can learn both cyclic and acyclic graphs and hence, unifies the task of experiment design for both types of graphs. We provide a lower bound on the number of experiments required to guarantee the unique identification of the causal graph in the worst case, showing that the proposed approach is order-optimal in terms of the number of experiments up to an additive logarithmic term. Moreover, we extend our result to the setting where the size of each experiment is bounded by a constant. For this case, we show that our approach is optimal in terms of the size of the largest experiment required for uniquely identifying the causal graph in the worst case.
abstract_zh: 我们研究实验设计，以唯一识别简单 SCM 的因果图，其中该图可能包含循环。结构中循环的存在给实验设计带来了重大挑战，因为与非循环图不同，仅从观察分布中学习具有循环的因果图的骨架可能是不可能的。此外，对此类图中的变量进行干预并不一定会导致所有与其关联的边定向。在本文中，我们提出了一种可以学习循环图和非循环图的实验设计方法，从而统一了两种类型图的实验设计任务。我们提供了保证在最坏情况下唯一识别因果图所需的实验数量的下限，表明所提出的方法在实验数量达到加性对数项方面是顺序最优的。此外，我们将结果扩展到每个实验的大小受常数限制的设置。对于这种情况，我们表明，就在最坏情况下唯一识别因果图所需的最大实验规模而言，我们的方法是最佳的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种统一的实验设计方法，用于唯一识别包含循环和非循环结构的因果图。
AI.motivation: 循环因果图的结构使得仅通过观测数据难以学习其骨架，且干预操作可能无法完全确定边方向，现有方法难以统一处理循环与非循环图。
AI.method: 提出一种适用于循环和非循环因果模型的实验设计框架，通过提供实验次数下界并证明其在最坏情况下的最优性，同时扩展至实验规模受限的情形。
AI.result: 证明了该方法在实验次数上达到最优（至多相差对数项），并展示了在实验规模受限时的最优性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法统一了循环与非循环因果图的实验设计任务，在实验次数和规模上均达到理论最优。

The presence of cycles in the structure introduces major challenges for experiment design as, unlike acyclic graphs, learning the skeleton of causal graphs with cycles may not be possible from merely the observational distribution. Furthermore, intervening on a variable in such graphs does not necessarily lead to orienting all the edges incident to it. In this paper, we propose an experiment design approach that can learn both cyclic and acyclic graphs and hence, unifies the task of experiment design for both types of graphs. Moreover, we extend our result to the setting where the size of each experiment is bounded by a constant. authors_text: Xiuxiu Bai,Zhe Liu,Yao Gao,Bin Liu,Yongqiang Hao
title: Emergence of Double-slit Interference by Representing Visual Space in Artificial Neural Networks
title_zh: 通过在人工神经网络中表示视觉空间来出现双缝干涉
abstract: Artificial neural networks have realized incredible successes at image recognition, but the underlying mechanism of visual space representation remains a huge mystery. Grid cells (2014 Nobel Prize) in the entorhinal cortex support a periodic representation as a metric for coding space. Here, we develop a self-supervised convolutional neural network to perform visual space location, leading to the emergence of single-slit diffraction and double-slit interference patterns of waves. Our discoveries reveal the nature of CNN encoding visual space to a certain extent. CNN is no longer a black box in terms of visual spatial encoding, it is interpretable. Our findings indicate that the periodicity property of waves provides a space metric, suggesting a general role of spatial coordinate frame in artificial neural networks.
summary: Artificial neural networks have realized incredible successes at image recognition, but the underlying mechanism of visual space representation remains a huge mystery. Grid cells (2014 Nobel Prize) in the entorhinal cortex support a periodic representation as a metric for coding space. Here, we develop a self-supervised convolutional neural network to perform visual space location, leading to the emergence of single-slit diffraction and double-slit interference patterns of waves. Our discoveries reveal the nature of CNN encoding visual space to a certain extent. CNN is no longer a black box in terms of visual spatial encoding, it is interpretable. Our findings indicate that the periodicity property of waves provides a space metric, suggesting a general role of spatial coordinate frame in artificial neural networks.
abstract_zh: 人工神经网络在图像识别方面取得了令人难以置信的成功，但视觉空间表示的基本机制仍然是一个巨大的谜团。内嗅皮层中的网格细胞（2014 年诺贝尔奖）支持周期表示作为编码空间的度量。在这里，我们开发了一种自监督卷积神经网络来执行视觉空间定位，从而导致单缝衍射和双缝干涉波图案的出现。我们的发现在一定程度上揭示了 CNN 编码视觉空间的本质。 CNN 在视觉空间编码方面不再是一个黑匣子，它是可解释的。我们的研究结果表明，波的周期性特性提供了空间度量，表明空间坐标系在人工神经网络中的一般作用。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过人工神经网络揭示视觉空间表示机制，发现卷积神经网络在处理视觉空间任务时能产生类似波动的衍射和干涉图样。
AI.motivation: 尽管卷积神经网络在图像识别中取得成功，但其如何表征视觉空间的机制仍不清楚。受生物体网格细胞周期性空间编码启发，研究旨在探索CNN的视觉空间表示本质。
AI.method: 开发自监督卷积神经网络进行视觉空间定位任务，通过分析网络激活模式揭示其空间表征特性。
AI.result: 实验观察到CNN中出现单缝衍射和双缝干涉模式，证明网络通过周期性特征提取实现空间度量，使视觉空间编码可解释。
AI.conclusion: CNN的视觉空间编码具有可解释性，波动的周期性特性为人工神经网络提供了空间坐标框架的通用机制。

authors_text: John Hartley,Sotirios A. Tsaftaris
title: Unintended memorisation of unique features in neural networks
title_zh: 无意中记住神经网络中的独特特征
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.08099
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.08099
abstract: Neural networks pose a privacy risk due to their propensity to memorise and leak training data. We show that unique features occurring only once in training data are memorised by discriminative multi-layer perceptrons and convolutional neural networks trained on benchmark imaging datasets. We design our method for settings where sensitive training data is not available, for example medical imaging. Our setting knows the unique feature, but not the training data, model weights or the unique feature's label. We develop a score estimating a model's sensitivity to a unique feature by comparing the KL divergences of the model's output distributions given modified out-of-distribution images. We find that typical strategies to prevent overfitting do not prevent unique feature memorisation. And that images containing a unique feature are highly influential, regardless of the influence the images's other features. We also find a significant variation in memorisation with training seed. These results imply that neural networks pose a privacy risk to rarely occurring private information. This risk is more pronounced in healthcare applications since sensitive patient information can be memorised when it remains in training data due to an imperfect data sanitisation process.
summary: Neural networks pose a privacy risk due to their propensity to memorise and leak training data. We show that unique features occurring only once in training data are memorised by discriminative multi-layer perceptrons and convolutional neural networks trained on benchmark imaging datasets. We design our method for settings where sensitive training data is not available, for example medical imaging. Our setting knows the unique feature, but not the training data, model weights or the unique feature's label. We develop a score estimating a model's sensitivity to a unique feature by comparing the KL divergences of the model's output distributions given modified out-of-distribution images. We find that typical strategies to prevent overfitting do not prevent unique feature memorisation. And that images containing a unique feature are highly influential, regardless of the influence the images's other features. We also find a significant variation in memorisation with training seed. These results imply that neural networks pose a privacy risk to rarely occurring private information. This risk is more pronounced in healthcare applications since sensitive patient information can be memorised when it remains in training data due to an imperfect data sanitisation process.
abstract_zh: 神经网络由于倾向于记忆和泄露训练数据而带来隐私风险。我们表明，训练数据中仅出现一次的独特特征可以通过在基准成像数据集上训练的判别性多层感知器和卷积神经网络来记忆。我们为无法获得敏感训练数据的环境（例如医学成像）设计方法。我们的设置知道独特的特征，但不知道训练数据、模型权重或独特特征的标签。我们通过比较给定修改后的分布外图像的模型输出分布的 KL 散度，开发了一个分数来估计模型对独特特征的敏感性。我们发现防止过度拟合的典型策略并不能阻止独特特征的记忆。并且，无论图像的其他特征的影响如何，包含独特特征的图像都具有很高的影响力。我们还发现训练种子的记忆力存在显着差异。这些结果意味着神经网络对很少出现的私人信息构成了隐私风险。这种风险在医疗保健应用中更为明显，因为由于数据清理过程不完善，敏感的患者信息在保留在训练数据中时可能会被记住。

AI.tldr: 本文研究神经网络对训练数据中独特特征的意外记忆现象，特别是在隐私敏感场景下的风险。
AI.motivation: 神经网络可能记忆并泄露训练数据中的隐私信息，而医疗等领域的数据清洗不完善会加剧这一风险。
AI.method: 通过比较模型在修改后的分布外图像上的输出分布KL散度，评估模型对已知独特特征的敏感性。
AI.result: 发现防过拟合策略无法阻止独特特征记忆，含独特特征的图像具有高影响力，且训练种子显著影响记忆效果。
AI.conclusion: 神经网络对罕见私有信息存在隐私风险，医疗等场景需特别注意数据泄露隐患。

search_text: Unintended memorisation of unique features in neural networks 无意中记住神经网络中的独特特征 Neural networks pose a privacy risk due to their propensity to memorise and leak training data. We show that unique features occurring only once in training data are memorised by discriminative multi-layer perceptrons and convolutional neural networks trained on benchmark imaging datasets. We design our method for settings where sensitive training data is not available, for example medical imaging. Our setting knows the unique feature, but not the training data, model weights or the unique feature's label. We develop a score estimating a model's sensitivity to a unique feature by comparing the KL divergences of the model's output distributions given modified out-of-distribution images. We find that typical strategies to prevent overfitting do not prevent unique feature memorisation. And that images containing a unique feature are highly influential, regardless of the influence the images's other features. We also find a significant variation in memorisation with training seed. These results imply that neural networks pose a privacy risk to rarely occurring private information. This risk is more pronounced in healthcare applications since sensitive patient information can be memorised when it remains in training data due to an imperfect data sanitisation process. 神经网络由于倾向于记忆和泄露训练数据而带来隐私风险。我们表明，训练数据中仅出现一次的独特特征可以通过在基准成像数据集上训练的判别性多层感知器和卷积神经网络来记忆。我们为无法获得敏感训练数据的环境（例如医学成像）设计方法。我们的设置知道独特的特征，但不知道训练数据、模型权重或独特特征的标签。我们通过比较给定修改后的分布外图像的模型输出分布的 KL 散度，开发了一个分数来估计模型对独特特征的敏感性。我们发现防止过度拟合的典型策略并不能阻止独特特征的记忆。并且，无论图像的其他特征的影响如何，包含独特特征的图像都具有很高的影响力。我们还发现训练种子的记忆力存在显着差异。这些结果意味着神经网络对很少出现的私人信息构成了隐私风险。这种风险在医疗保健应用中更为明显，因为由于数据清理过程不完善，敏感的患者信息在保留在训练数据中时可能会被记住。 本文研究神经网络对训练数据中独特特征的意外记忆现象，特别是在隐私敏感场景下的风险。 John Hartley,Sotirios A. Tsaftaris cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Razvan Brinzea,Bulat Khaertdinov,Stylianos Asteriadis
title: Contrastive Learning with Cross-Modal Knowledge Mining for Multimodal Human Activity Recognition
title_zh: 用于多模态人类活动识别的跨模态知识挖掘的对比学习
comment: to be published in IEEE WCCI 2022 (IJCNN 2022 track)
comments: to be published in IEEE WCCI 2022 (IJCNN 2022 track)
abstract: Human Activity Recognition is a field of research where input data can take many forms. Each of the possible input modalities describes human behaviour in a different way, and each has its own strengths and weaknesses. We explore the hypothesis that leveraging multiple modalities can lead to better recognition. Since manual annotation of input data is expensive and time-consuming, the emphasis is made on self-supervised methods which can learn useful feature representations without any ground truth labels. We extend a number of recent contrastive self-supervised approaches for the task of Human Activity Recognition, leveraging inertial and skeleton data. Furthermore, we propose a flexible, general-purpose framework for performing multimodal self-supervised learning, named Contrastive Multiview Coding with Cross-Modal Knowledge Mining (CMC-CMKM). This framework exploits modality-specific knowledge in order to mitigate the limitations of typical self-supervised frameworks. The extensive experiments on two widely-used datasets demonstrate that the suggested framework significantly outperforms contrastive unimodal and multimodal baselines on different scenarios, including fully-supervised fine-tuning, activity retrieval and semi-supervised learning. Furthermore, it shows performance competitive even compared to supervised methods.
summary: Human Activity Recognition is a field of research where input data can take many forms. Each of the possible input modalities describes human behaviour in a different way, and each has its own strengths and weaknesses. We explore the hypothesis that leveraging multiple modalities can lead to better recognition. Since manual annotation of input data is expensive and time-consuming, the emphasis is made on self-supervised methods which can learn useful feature representations without any ground truth labels. We extend a number of recent contrastive self-supervised approaches for the task of Human Activity Recognition, leveraging inertial and skeleton data. Furthermore, we propose a flexible, general-purpose framework for performing multimodal self-supervised learning, named Contrastive Multiview Coding with Cross-Modal Knowledge Mining (CMC-CMKM). This framework exploits modality-specific knowledge in order to mitigate the limitations of typical self-supervised frameworks. The extensive experiments on two widely-used datasets demonstrate that the suggested framework significantly outperforms contrastive unimodal and multimodal baselines on different scenarios, including fully-supervised fine-tuning, activity retrieval and semi-supervised learning. Furthermore, it shows performance competitive even compared to supervised methods.
abstract_zh: 人类活动识别是一个输入数据可以采取多种形式的研究领域。每种可能的输入方式都以不同的方式描述人类行为，并且每种方式都有自己的优点和缺点。我们探讨了这样一个假设：利用多种模式可以带来更好的识别。由于输入数据的手动注释既昂贵又耗时，因此重点放在自我监督方法上，这种方法可以在没有任何真实标签的情况下学习有用的特征表示。我们利用惯性和骨架数据，扩展了许多最近用于人类活动识别任务的对比自监督方法。此外，我们提出了一种灵活的通用框架，用于执行多模态自监督学习，称为跨模态知识挖掘对比多视图编码（CMC-CMKM）。该框架利用特定模态的知识来减轻典型自我监督框架的局限性。对两个广泛使用的数据集进行的广泛实验表明，所建议的框架在不同场景下显着优于对比单模态和多模态基线，包括全监督微调、活动检索和半监督学习。此外，即使与监督方法相比，它也显示出具有竞争力的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于对比学习和跨模态知识挖掘的多模态人类活动识别框架CMC-CMKM，旨在通过融合惯性与骨骼数据提升识别效果。
AI.motivation: 传统人工标注数据成本高昂，且单一模态存在局限性，需探索无需标注的自监督方法以充分利用多模态数据优势。
AI.method: 扩展对比自监督方法，设计CMC-CMKM框架，通过跨模态知识挖掘利用不同模态的特性，缓解典型自监督框架的不足。
AI.result: 在两个常用数据集上，该框架在全监督微调、活动检索和半监督学习场景中显著优于单模态和多模态基线，性能接近监督方法。
AI.conclusion: CMC-CMKM通过跨模态知识挖掘有效提升了多模态活动识别性能，为减少对标注数据依赖提供了可行方案。

authors_text: Wenxuan Wang,Wenxiang Jiao,Shuo Wang,Zhaopeng Tu,Michael R. Lyu
title: Understanding and Mitigating the Uncertainty in Zero-Shot Translation
title_zh: 理解和减轻零样本翻译中的不确定性
comment: Accepted by The IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing (TASLP)
comments: Accepted by The IEEE/ACM Transactions on Audio, Speech, and Language Processing (TASLP)
abstract: Zero-shot translation is a promising direction for building a comprehensive multilingual neural machine translation~(MNMT) system. However, its quality is still not satisfactory due to off-target issues. In this paper, we aim to understand and alleviate the off-target issues from the perspective of uncertainty in zero-shot translation. By carefully examining the translation output and model confidence, we identify two uncertainties that are responsible for the off-target issues, namely, extrinsic data uncertainty and intrinsic model uncertainty. Based on the observations, we propose two lightweight and complementary approaches to denoise the training data for model training and explicitly penalize the off-target translations by unlikelihood training during model training. Extensive experiments on both balanced and imbalanced datasets show that our approaches significantly improve the performance of zero-shot translation over strong MNMT baselines.
summary: Zero-shot translation is a promising direction for building a comprehensive multilingual neural machine translation~(MNMT) system. However, its quality is still not satisfactory due to off-target issues. In this paper, we aim to understand and alleviate the off-target issues from the perspective of uncertainty in zero-shot translation. By carefully examining the translation output and model confidence, we identify two uncertainties that are responsible for the off-target issues, namely, extrinsic data uncertainty and intrinsic model uncertainty. Based on the observations, we propose two lightweight and complementary approaches to denoise the training data for model training and explicitly penalize the off-target translations by unlikelihood training during model training. Extensive experiments on both balanced and imbalanced datasets show that our approaches significantly improve the performance of zero-shot translation over strong MNMT baselines.
abstract_zh: 零样本翻译是构建综合多语言神经机器翻译（MNMT）系统的一个有前途的方向。然而，由于脱靶问题，其质量仍然不能令人满意。在本文中，我们旨在从零样本翻译的不确定性角度理解和缓解脱靶问题。通过仔细检查翻译输出和模型置信度，我们确定了导致脱靶问题的两个不确定性，即外在数据不确定性和内在模型不确定性。根据观察结果，我们提出了两种轻量级且互补的方法来对模型训练的训练数据进行去噪，并在模型训练期间通过不太可能训练来明确惩罚脱靶翻译。对平衡和不平衡数据集的大量实验表明，我们的方法在强 MNMT 基线上显着提高了零样本翻译的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在理解和缓解零样本翻译中的不确定性问题，以提高翻译质量。
AI.motivation: 零样本翻译面临off-target问题，导致翻译质量不高，需从不确定性角度进行改进。
AI.method: 提出两种轻量方法：数据去噪和非可能性训练，以减少不确定性影响。
AI.result: 实验显示，方法在多个数据集上显著提升零样本翻译性能，优于现有基线模型。
AI.conclusion: 通过分析并缓解不确定性，本文有效提升了零样本翻译的准确性和鲁棒性。

conclusion: 通过分析并缓解不确定性，本文有效提升了零样本翻译的准确性和鲁棒性。
search_text: Understanding and Mitigating the Uncertainty in Zero-Shot Translation 理解和减轻零样本翻译中的不确定性 Zero-shot translation is a promising direction for building a comprehensive multilingual neural machine translation~(MNMT) system. However, its quality is still not satisfactory due to off-target issues. In this paper, we aim to understand and alleviate the off-target issues from the perspective of uncertainty in zero-shot translation. By carefully examining the translation output and model confidence, we identify two uncertainties that are responsible for the off-target issues, namely, extrinsic data uncertainty and intrinsic model uncertainty. Based on the observations, we propose two lightweight and complementary approaches to denoise the training data for model training and explicitly penalize the off-target translations by unlikelihood training during model training. Extensive experiments on both balanced and imbalanced datasets show that our approaches significantly improve the performance of zero-shot translation over strong MNMT baselines. 零样本翻译是构建综合多语言神经机器翻译（MNMT）系统的一个有前途的方向。然而，由于脱靶问题，其质量仍然不能令人满意。在本文中，我们旨在从零样本翻译的不确定性角度理解和缓解脱靶问题。通过仔细检查翻译输出和模型置信度，我们确定了导致脱靶问题的两个不确定性，即外在数据不确定性和内在模型不确定性。根据观察结果，我们提出了两种轻量级且互补的方法来对模型训练的训练数据进行去噪，并在模型训练期间通过不太可能训练来明确惩罚脱靶翻译。对平衡和不平衡数据集的大量实验表明，我们的方法在强 MNMT 基线上显着提高了零样本翻译的性能。 本文旨在理解和缓解零样本翻译中的不确定性问题，以提高翻译质量。 Wenxuan Wang,Wenxiang Jiao,Shuo Wang,Zhaopeng Tu,Michael R. Lyu cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Xiang Li,Wenhai Wang,Lingfeng Yang,Jian Yang
title: Uniform Masking: Enabling MAE Pre-training for Pyramid-based Vision Transformers with Locality
title_zh: 统一掩蔽：为具有局部性的基于金字塔的视觉变换器启用 MAE 预训练
comment: An efficient and effective technique that supports MAE-style MIM Pre-training for popular Pyramid-based Vision Transformers (e.g., PVT, Swin)
comments: An efficient and effective technique that supports MAE-style MIM Pre-training for popular Pyramid-based Vision Transformers (e.g., PVT, Swin)
abstract: Masked AutoEncoder (MAE) has recently led the trends of visual self-supervision area by an elegant asymmetric encoder-decoder design, which significantly optimizes both the pre-training efficiency and fine-tuning accuracy. Notably, the success of the asymmetric structure relies on the "global" property of Vanilla Vision Transformer (ViT), whose self-attention mechanism reasons over arbitrary subset of discrete image patches. However, it is still unclear how the advanced Pyramid-based ViTs (e.g., PVT, Swin) can be adopted in MAE pre-training as they commonly introduce operators within "local" windows, making it difficult to handle the random sequence of partial vision tokens. In this paper, we propose Uniform Masking (UM), successfully enabling MAE pre-training for Pyramid-based ViTs with locality (termed "UM-MAE" for short). Specifically, UM includes a Uniform Sampling (US) that strictly samples $1$ random patch from each $2 \times 2$ grid, and a Secondary Masking (SM) which randomly masks a portion of (usually $25\%$) the already sampled regions as learnable tokens. US preserves equivalent elements across multiple non-overlapped local windows, resulting in the smooth support for popular Pyramid-based ViTs; whilst SM is designed for better transferable visual representations since US reduces the difficulty of pixel recovery pre-task that hinders the semantic learning. We demonstrate that UM-MAE significantly improves the pre-training efficiency (e.g., it speeds up and reduces the GPU memory by $\sim 2\times$) of Pyramid-based ViTs, but maintains the competitive fine-tuning performance across downstream tasks. For example using HTC++ detector, the pre-trained Swin-Large backbone self-supervised under UM-MAE only in ImageNet-1K can even outperform the one supervised in ImageNet-22K. The codes are available at https://github.com/implus/UM-MAE.

abstract_zh: Masked AutoEncoder（MAE）最近通过优雅的非对称编码器-解码器设计引领了视觉自监督领域的趋势，显着优化了预训练效率和微调精度。值得注意的是，非对称结构的成功依赖于 Vanilla Vision Transformer (ViT) 的“全局”属性，其自注意力机制对离散图像块的任意子集进行推理。然而，目前尚不清楚如何在 MAE 预训练中采用先进的基于 Pyramid 的 ViT（例如 PVT、Swin），因为它们通常在“本地”窗口内引入算子，使得处理部分视觉标记的随机序列变得困难。在本文中，我们提出了统一掩码（UM），成功地实现了具有局部性的基于金字塔的 ViT 的 MAE 预训练（简称“UM-MAE”）。具体来说，UM 包括一个均匀采样（US），它从每个 $2 × 2$ 网格中严格采样 $1$ 随机补丁，以及一个二次掩蔽（SM），它随机掩蔽已采样区域的一部分（通常是 $25\%$）作为可学习标记。 US 在多个不重叠的本地窗口中保留等效元素，从而顺利支持流行的基于 Pyramid 的 ViT；而 SM 是为了更好的可转移视觉表示而设计的，因为 US 降低了阻碍语义学习的像素恢复前置任务的难度。我们证明，UM-MAE 显着提高了基于 Pyramid 的 ViT 的预训练效率（例如，它加速并减少了 GPU 内存 $\sim 2\times$），但在下游任务中保持了有竞争力的微调性能。例如，使用 HTC++ 检测器，仅在 ImageNet-1K 中在 UM-MAE 下进行自我监督的预训练 Swin-Large 骨干网甚至可以优于 ImageNet-22K 中的监督骨干网。这些代码可从 https://github.com/implus/UM-MAE 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Uniform Masking（UM）的方法，使基于金字塔结构的视觉Transformer（如PVT、Swin）能够有效进行MAE预训练。
AI.motivation: 现有的MAE预训练依赖于ViT的全局特性，但金字塔结构的ViT引入了局部窗口操作，难以处理随机部分视觉标记，限制了其在MAE中的应用。
AI.method: UM包含两个关键步骤：Uniform Sampling（US）从每个2×2网格中严格采样一个随机块，Secondary Masking（SM）对已采样区域的25%进行二次掩码，以增强语义学习。
AI.result: UM-MAE显著提升了金字塔ViT的预训练效率（速度和内存消耗降低约2倍），并在下游任务中保持竞争力，例如在ImageNet-1K自监督预训练的Swin-Large模型性能优于ImageNet-22K监督模型。
AI.conclusion: UM通过统一采样和二次掩码机制，成功解决了局部窗口ViT在MAE中的适配问题，为高效预训练提供了新思路。

For example using HTC++ detector, the pre-trained Swin-Large backbone self-supervised under UM-MAE only in ImageNet-1K can even outperform the one supervised in ImageNet-22K.
