authors_text: Nis Meinert,Jakob Gawlikowski,Alexander Lavin
title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Deep Evidential Regression
title_zh: 深度证据回归的不合理有效性
abstract: There is a significant need for principled uncertainty reasoning in machine learning systems as they are increasingly deployed in safety-critical domains. A new approach with uncertainty-aware regression-based neural networks (NNs), based on learning evidential distributions for aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties, shows promise over traditional deterministic methods and typical Bayesian NNs, notably with the capabilities to disentangle aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties. Despite some empirical success of Deep Evidential Regression (DER), there are important gaps in the mathematical foundation that raise the question of why the proposed technique seemingly works. We detail the theoretical shortcomings and analyze the performance on synthetic and real-world data sets, showing that Deep Evidential Regression is a heuristic rather than an exact uncertainty quantification. We go on to discuss corrections and redefinitions of how aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties should be extracted from NNs.
summary: There is a significant need for principled uncertainty reasoning in machine learning systems as they are increasingly deployed in safety-critical domains. A new approach with uncertainty-aware regression-based neural networks (NNs), based on learning evidential distributions for aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties, shows promise over traditional deterministic methods and typical Bayesian NNs, notably with the capabilities to disentangle aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties. Despite some empirical success of Deep Evidential Regression (DER), there are important gaps in the mathematical foundation that raise the question of why the proposed technique seemingly works. We detail the theoretical shortcomings and analyze the performance on synthetic and real-world data sets, showing that Deep Evidential Regression is a heuristic rather than an exact uncertainty quantification. We go on to discuss corrections and redefinitions of how aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties should be extracted from NNs.
abstract_zh: 随着机器学习系统越来越多地部署在安全关键领域，机器学习系统迫切需要原则性的不确定性推理。一种基于不确定性感知回归的神经网络（NN）的新方法，基于学习任意和认知不确定性的证据分布，显示出优于传统确定性方法和典型贝叶斯神经网络的前景，特别是具有解开任意和认知不确定性的能力。尽管深度证据回归（DER）取得了一些经验上的成功，但数学基础上存在重要差距，引发了为什么所提出的技术看似有效的问题。我们详细介绍了理论缺陷，并分析了在合成数据集和现实世界数据集上的性能，表明深度证据回归是一种启发式方法，而不是精确的不确定性量化。我们继续讨论如何从神经网络中提取任意和认知不确定性的修正和重新定义。

AI.tldr: 分析深度证据回归（DER）的理论缺陷，并评估其在合成和真实数据集上的表现，指出其作为启发式方法的局限性。
AI.motivation: 在安全关键领域中，机器学习系统需要可靠地处理不确定性，而现有方法如传统确定性模型和贝叶斯神经网络存在不足，需探索更有效的不确定性量化方法。
AI.method: 通过理论分析和实证评估，探讨DER的数学基础缺陷，并在合成及真实数据集上验证其性能，提出修正的不确定性提取方法。
AI.result: 实验表明，DER在不确定性量化上存在理论缺陷，其效果依赖于特定假设，实际应用中可能不准确。
AI.conclusion: 深度证据回归虽有效，但缺乏理论严谨性，需改进不确定性分解方法以提高可靠性。

We go on to discuss corrections and redefinitions of how aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties should be extracted from NNs. 随着机器学习系统越来越多地部署在安全关键领域，机器学习系统迫切需要原则性的不确定性推理。一种基于不确定性感知回归的神经网络（NN）的新方法，基于学习任意和认知不确定性的证据分布，显示出优于传统确定性方法和典型贝叶斯神经网络的前景，特别是具有解开任意和认知不确定性的能力。尽管深度证据回归（DER）取得了一些经验上的成功，但数学基础上存在重要差距，引发了为什么所提出的技术看似有效的问题。我们详细介绍了理论缺陷，并分析了在合成数据集和现实世界数据集上的性能，表明深度证据回归是一种启发式方法，而不是精确的不确定性量化。我们继续讨论如何从神经网络中提取任意和认知不确定性的修正和重新定义。 分析深度证据回归（DER）的理论缺陷，并评估其在合成和真实数据集上的表现，指出其作为启发式方法的局限性。 Nis Meinert,Jakob Gawlikowski,Alexander Lavin cs.LG,cs.NE,stat.ML

authors_text: Vivien Cabannes,Stefano Vigogna
title: A Case of Exponential Convergence Rates for SVM
title_zh: SVM 指数收敛率的一个案例
abstract: Classification is often the first problem described in introductory machine learning classes. Generalization guarantees of classification have historically been offered by Vapnik-Chervonenkis theory. Yet those guarantees are based on intractable algorithms, which has led to the theory of surrogate methods in classification. Guarantees offered by surrogate methods are based on calibration inequalities, which have been shown to be highly sub-optimal under some margin conditions, failing short to capture exponential convergence phenomena. Those "super" fast rates are becoming to be well understood for smooth surrogates, but the picture remains blurry for non-smooth losses such as the hinge loss, associated with the renowned support vector machines. In this paper, we present a simple mechanism to obtain fast convergence rates and we investigate its usage for SVM. In particular, we show that SVM can exhibit exponential convergence rates even without assuming the hard Tsybakov margin condition.
summary: Classification is often the first problem described in introductory machine learning classes. Generalization guarantees of classification have historically been offered by Vapnik-Chervonenkis theory. Yet those guarantees are based on intractable algorithms, which has led to the theory of surrogate methods in classification. Guarantees offered by surrogate methods are based on calibration inequalities, which have been shown to be highly sub-optimal under some margin conditions, failing short to capture exponential convergence phenomena. Those "super" fast rates are becoming to be well understood for smooth surrogates, but the picture remains blurry for non-smooth losses such as the hinge loss, associated with the renowned support vector machines. In this paper, we present a simple mechanism to obtain fast convergence rates and we investigate its usage for SVM. In particular, we show that SVM can exhibit exponential convergence rates even without assuming the hard Tsybakov margin condition.
abstract_zh: 分类通常是机器学习入门课程中描述的第一个问题。历史上，Vapnik-Chervonenkis 理论提供了分类的泛化保证。然而，这些保证是基于棘手的算法，这导致了分类中替代方法理论的出现。替代方法提供的保证基于校准不等式，事实证明，在某些裕度条件下，替代方法是高度次优的，无法捕获指数收敛现象。对于平滑代理来说，这些“超”快的速率已经被很好地理解，但对于非平滑损失（例如与著名的支持向量机相关的铰链损失），情况仍然模糊。在本文中，我们提出了一种获得快速收敛速度的简单机制，并研究了其在 SVM 中的用法。特别是，我们证明即使不假设硬 Tsybakov 裕度条件，SVM 也可以表现出指数收敛速度。

AI.tldr: 本文研究支持向量机（SVM）在非光滑损失函数下的指数收敛速率问题，提出了一种无需假设硬Tsybakov边缘条件的快速收敛机制。
AI.motivation: 传统分类理论依赖难以处理的算法，而替代方法的校准不等式在非光滑损失（如铰链损失）下无法有效捕捉指数收敛现象，SVM的收敛速率研究仍存在理论空白。
AI.method: 提出一种简单机制分析SVM的收敛性，通过理论推导证明其在非光滑损失下的快速收敛特性。
AI.result: 证明SVM在无需硬Tsybakov边缘条件假设的情况下，仍可实现指数级收敛速率。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为SVM的理论分析提供了新视角，揭示了其在非光滑损失下可能存在的超快速收敛特性，扩展了传统理论的适用范围。

authors_text: Jintang Li,Ruofan Wu,Wangbin Sun,Liang Chen,Sheng Tian,Liang Zhu,Changhua Meng,Zibin Zheng,Weiqiang Wang
title: What's Behind the Mask: Understanding Masked Graph Modeling for Graph Autoencoders
title_zh: 掩码背后是什么：了解图自动编码器的掩码图建模
comment: KDD 2023 research track. Code available at https://github.com/EdisonLeeeee/MaskGAE
comments: KDD 2023 research track. Code available at https://github.com/EdisonLeeeee/MaskGAE
abstract: The last years have witnessed the emergence of a promising self-supervised learning strategy, referred to as masked autoencoding. However, there is a lack of theoretical understanding of how masking matters on graph autoencoders (GAEs). In this work, we present masked graph autoencoder (MaskGAE), a self-supervised learning framework for graph-structured data. Different from standard GAEs, MaskGAE adopts masked graph modeling (MGM) as a principled pretext task - masking a portion of edges and attempting to reconstruct the missing part with partially visible, unmasked graph structure. To understand whether MGM can help GAEs learn better representations, we provide both theoretical and empirical evidence to comprehensively justify the benefits of this pretext task. Theoretically, we establish close connections between GAEs and contrastive learning, showing that MGM significantly improves the self-supervised learning scheme of GAEs. Empirically, we conduct extensive experiments on a variety of graph benchmarks, demonstrating the superiority of MaskGAE over several state-of-the-arts on both link prediction and node classification tasks.
summary: The last years have witnessed the emergence of a promising self-supervised learning strategy, referred to as masked autoencoding. However, there is a lack of theoretical understanding of how masking matters on graph autoencoders (GAEs). In this work, we present masked graph autoencoder (MaskGAE), a self-supervised learning framework for graph-structured data. Different from standard GAEs, MaskGAE adopts masked graph modeling (MGM) as a principled pretext task - masking a portion of edges and attempting to reconstruct the missing part with partially visible, unmasked graph structure. To understand whether MGM can help GAEs learn better representations, we provide both theoretical and empirical evidence to comprehensively justify the benefits of this pretext task. Theoretically, we establish close connections between GAEs and contrastive learning, showing that MGM significantly improves the self-supervised learning scheme of GAEs. Empirically, we conduct extensive experiments on a variety of graph benchmarks, demonstrating the superiority of MaskGAE over several state-of-the-arts on both link prediction and node classification tasks.
abstract_zh: 在过去的几年里，出现了一种有前途的自我监督学习策略，称为屏蔽自动编码。然而，对于掩码对图自动编码器（GAE）的重要性缺乏理论理解。在这项工作中，我们提出了掩码图自动编码器（MaskGAE），这是一种用于图结构数据的自监督学习框架。与标准 GAE 不同，MaskGAE 采用掩蔽图建模（MGM）作为原则借口任务 - 掩蔽一部分边缘并尝试用部分可见、未掩蔽的图结构重建丢失的部分。为了了解 MGM 是否可以帮助 GAE 学习更好的表示，我们提供了理论和经验证据来全面证明这一借口任务的好处。理论上，我们在 GAE 和对比学习之间建立了密切的联系，表明 MGM 显着改进了 GAE 的自监督学习方案。根据经验，我们对各种图基准进行了广泛的实验，证明了 MaskGAE 在链路预测和节点分类任务方面优于几种最先进的技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于掩码图建模的自监督学习框架MaskGAE，用于提升图结构数据的表示学习能力，并通过理论分析和实验验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有图自编码器（GAEs）缺乏对掩码机制的理论理解，而掩码自编码在图像/文本领域表现优异，需探索其在图结构数据中的适用性及理论依据。
AI.method: 设计MaskGAE框架，通过遮蔽图中部分边并重建缺失部分作为预训练任务，建立GAEs与对比学习的理论联系，结合实验验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 在多个图基准数据集上，MaskGAE在链接预测和节点分类任务中均优于现有先进方法，验证了掩码图建模的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 掩码图建模能显著提升GAEs的表示学习能力，理论分析与实验结果共同证明了该方法的优越性，为图结构数据的自监督学习提供了新方向。

authors_text: Haokai Hong,Min Jiang,Liang Feng,Qiuzhen Lin,Kay Chen Tan
title: Balancing Exploration and Exploitation for Solving Large-scale Multiobjective Optimization via Attention Mechanism
title_zh: 通过注意力机制平衡探索和利用来解决大规模多目标优化
abstract: Large-scale multiobjective optimization problems (LSMOPs) refer to optimization problems with multiple conflicting optimization objectives and hundreds or even thousands of decision variables. A key point in solving LSMOPs is how to balance exploration and exploitation so that the algorithm can search in a huge decision space efficiently. Large-scale multiobjective evolutionary algorithms consider the balance between exploration and exploitation from the individual's perspective. However, these algorithms ignore the significance of tackling this issue from the perspective of decision variables, which makes the algorithm lack the ability to search from different dimensions and limits the performance of the algorithm. In this paper, we propose a large-scale multiobjective optimization algorithm based on the attention mechanism, called (LMOAM). The attention mechanism will assign a unique weight to each decision variable, and LMOAM will use this weight to strike a balance between exploration and exploitation from the decision variable level. Nine different sets of LSMOP benchmarks are conducted to verify the algorithm proposed in this paper, and the experimental results validate the effectiveness of our design.
summary: Large-scale multiobjective optimization problems (LSMOPs) refer to optimization problems with multiple conflicting optimization objectives and hundreds or even thousands of decision variables. A key point in solving LSMOPs is how to balance exploration and exploitation so that the algorithm can search in a huge decision space efficiently. Large-scale multiobjective evolutionary algorithms consider the balance between exploration and exploitation from the individual's perspective. However, these algorithms ignore the significance of tackling this issue from the perspective of decision variables, which makes the algorithm lack the ability to search from different dimensions and limits the performance of the algorithm. In this paper, we propose a large-scale multiobjective optimization algorithm based on the attention mechanism, called (LMOAM). The attention mechanism will assign a unique weight to each decision variable, and LMOAM will use this weight to strike a balance between exploration and exploitation from the decision variable level. Nine different sets of LSMOP benchmarks are conducted to verify the algorithm proposed in this paper, and the experimental results validate the effectiveness of our design.
abstract_zh: 大规模多目标优化问题（LSMOP）是指具有多个相互冲突的优化目标和数百甚至数千个决策变量的优化问题。解决LSMOPs的一个关键点是如何平衡探索和利用，使算法能够在巨大的决策空间中高效地搜索。大规模多目标进化算法从个体的角度考虑探索和利用之间的平衡。然而这些算法忽略了从决策变量的角度解决这一问题的意义，使得算法缺乏从不同维度进行搜索的能力，限制了算法的性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于注意力机制的大规模多目标优化算法，称为（LMOAM）。注意力机制会给每个决策变量分配一个唯一的权重，LMOAM将利用这个权重从决策变量层面在探索和利用之间取得平衡。我们进行了九组不同的LSMOP基准来验证本文提出的算法，实验结果验证了我们设计的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于注意力机制的大规模多目标优化算法LMOAM，通过决策变量层面的权重分配平衡探索与利用，提升优化效率。
AI.motivation: 现有算法仅从个体角度平衡探索与利用，忽视决策变量维度的分析，导致搜索不同维度能力不足，限制算法性能。
AI.method: 设计LMOAM算法，利用注意力机制为每个决策变量分配唯一权重，在变量层面动态平衡探索与利用。
AI.result: 九组LSMOP基准测试验证了LMOAM的有效性，实验结果证明该方法在搜索效率和优化性能上优于现有算法。
AI.conclusion: 通过注意力机制从决策变量维度平衡探索与利用，显著提升了大规模多目标优化算法的性能，为解决LSMOPs提供新思路。

search_text: Balancing Exploration and Exploitation for Solving Large-scale Multiobjective Optimization via Attention Mechanism 通过注意力机制平衡探索和利用来解决大规模多目标优化 Large-scale multiobjective optimization problems (LSMOPs) refer to optimization problems with multiple conflicting optimization objectives and hundreds or even thousands of decision variables. A key point in solving LSMOPs is how to balance exploration and exploitation so that the algorithm can search in a huge decision space efficiently. Large-scale multiobjective evolutionary algorithms consider the balance between exploration and exploitation from the individual's perspective. However, these algorithms ignore the significance of tackling this issue from the perspective of decision variables, which makes the algorithm lack the ability to search from different dimensions and limits the performance of the algorithm. In this paper, we propose a large-scale multiobjective optimization algorithm based on the attention mechanism, called (LMOAM). The attention mechanism will assign a unique weight to each decision variable, and LMOAM will use this weight to strike a balance between exploration and exploitation from the decision variable level. Nine different sets of LSMOP benchmarks are conducted to verify the algorithm proposed in this paper, and the experimental results validate the effectiveness of our design. 大规模多目标优化问题（LSMOP）是指具有多个相互冲突的优化目标和数百甚至数千个决策变量的优化问题。解决LSMOPs的一个关键点是如何平衡探索和利用，使算法能够在巨大的决策空间中高效地搜索。大规模多目标进化算法从个体的角度考虑探索和利用之间的平衡。然而这些算法忽略了从决策变量的角度解决这一问题的意义，使得算法缺乏从不同维度进行搜索的能力，限制了算法的性能。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于注意力机制的大规模多目标优化算法，称为（LMOAM）。注意力机制会给每个决策变量分配一个唯一的权重，LMOAM将利用这个权重从决策变量层面在探索和利用之间取得平衡。我们进行了九组不同的LSMOP基准来验证本文提出的算法，实验结果验证了我们设计的有效性。 本文提出基于注意力机制的大规模多目标优化算法LMOAM，通过决策变量层面的权重分配平衡探索与利用，提升优化效率。 Haokai Hong,Min Jiang,Liang Feng,Qiuzhen Lin,Kay Chen Tan cs.NE

authors_text: Iris Dominguez-Catena,Daniel Paternain,Mikel Galar
title: Assessing Demographic Bias Transfer from Dataset to Model: A Case Study in Facial Expression Recognition
title_zh: 评估从数据集到模型的人口统计学偏差转移：面部表情识别案例研究
abstract: The increasing amount of applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has led researchers to study the social impact of these technologies and evaluate their fairness. Unfortunately, current fairness metrics are hard to apply in multi-class multi-demographic classification problems, such as Facial Expression Recognition (FER). We propose a new set of metrics to approach these problems. Of the three metrics proposed, two focus on the representational and stereotypical bias of the dataset, and the third one on the residual bias of the trained model. These metrics combined can potentially be used to study and compare diverse bias mitigation methods. We demonstrate the usefulness of the metrics by applying them to a FER problem based on the popular Affectnet dataset. Like many other datasets for FER, Affectnet is a large Internet-sourced dataset with 291,651 labeled images. Obtaining images from the Internet raises some concerns over the fairness of any system trained on this data and its ability to generalize properly to diverse populations. We first analyze the dataset and some variants, finding substantial racial bias and gender stereotypes. We then extract several subsets with different demographic properties and train a model on each one, observing the amount of residual bias in the different setups. We also provide a second analysis on a different dataset, FER+.
summary: The increasing amount of applications of Artificial Intelligence (AI) has led researchers to study the social impact of these technologies and evaluate their fairness. Unfortunately, current fairness metrics are hard to apply in multi-class multi-demographic classification problems, such as Facial Expression Recognition (FER). We propose a new set of metrics to approach these problems. Of the three metrics proposed, two focus on the representational and stereotypical bias of the dataset, and the third one on the residual bias of the trained model. These metrics combined can potentially be used to study and compare diverse bias mitigation methods. We demonstrate the usefulness of the metrics by applying them to a FER problem based on the popular Affectnet dataset. Like many other datasets for FER, Affectnet is a large Internet-sourced dataset with 291,651 labeled images. Obtaining images from the Internet raises some concerns over the fairness of any system trained on this data and its ability to generalize properly to diverse populations. We first analyze the dataset and some variants, finding substantial racial bias and gender stereotypes. We then extract several subsets with different demographic properties and train a model on each one, observing the amount of residual bias in the different setups. We also provide a second analysis on a different dataset, FER+.
abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）应用的不断增加促使研究人员研究这些技术的社会影响并评估其公平性。不幸的是，当前的公平性指标很难应用于多类多人口分类问题，例如面部表情识别（FER）。我们提出了一套新的指标来解决这些问题。在提出的三个指标中，两个关注数据集的代表性和刻板印象偏差，第三个关注训练模型的残余偏差。这些指标组合起来可用于研究和比较不同的偏差缓解方法。我们通过将这些指标应用于基于流行的 Affectnet 数据集的 FER 问题来展示这些指标的有用性。与 FER 的许多其他数据集一样，Affectnet 是一个来自互联网的大型数据集，包含 291,651 个标记图像。从互联网获取图像引起了人们对任何接受这些数据训练的系统的公平性及其正确推广到不同人群的能力的担忧。我们首先分析数据集和一些变体，发现大量的种族偏见和性别刻板印象。然后，我们提取具有不同人口统计特征的几个子集，并在每个子集上训练一个模型，观察不同设置中的残余偏差量。我们还提供了对不同数据集 FER+ 的第二次分析。

AI.tldr: 本文提出新的度量方法评估数据集到模型的人口统计偏差转移问题，并在面部表情识别任务中验证其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有公平性度量难以处理多类多人口统计分类问题，而面部表情识别等任务的数据集可能存在种族、性别等偏差，影响模型公平性。
AI.method: 设计三个指标：两个分析数据集的表示偏差与刻板印象偏差，一个评估模型残余偏差；基于Affectnet和FER+数据集进行实验验证。
AI.result: 发现Affectnet数据集存在显著种族与性别偏差，不同人口统计子集训练的模型残余偏差差异明显，FER+分析结果一致。
AI.conclusion: 提出的度量方法可有效分析偏差转移问题，为比较不同偏差缓解策略提供工具，需关注数据集公平性对模型的影响。

authors_text: Pradip Mainali,Ismini Psychoula,Fabien A. P. Petitcolas
title: ExMo: Explainable AI Model using Inverse Frequency Decision Rules
title_zh: ExMo：使用逆频率决策规则的可解释人工智能模型
abstract: In this paper, we present a novel method to compute decision rules to build a more accurate interpretable machine learning model, denoted as ExMo. The ExMo interpretable machine learning model consists of a list of IF...THEN... statements with a decision rule in the condition. This way, ExMo naturally provides an explanation for a prediction using the decision rule that was triggered. ExMo uses a new approach to extract decision rules from the training data using term frequency-inverse document frequency (TF-IDF) features. With TF-IDF, decision rules with feature values that are more relevant to each class are extracted. Hence, the decision rules obtained by ExMo can distinguish the positive and negative classes better than the decision rules used in the existing Bayesian Rule List (BRL) algorithm, obtained using the frequent pattern mining approach. The paper also shows that ExMo learns a qualitatively better model than BRL. Furthermore, ExMo demonstrates that the textual explanation can be provided in a human-friendly way so that the explanation can be easily understood by non-expert users. We validate ExMo on several datasets with different sizes to evaluate its efficacy. Experimental validation on a real-world fraud detection application shows that ExMo is 20% more accurate than BRL and that it achieves accuracy similar to those of deep learning models.
summary: In this paper, we present a novel method to compute decision rules to build a more accurate interpretable machine learning model, denoted as ExMo. The ExMo interpretable machine learning model consists of a list of IF...THEN... statements with a decision rule in the condition. This way, ExMo naturally provides an explanation for a prediction using the decision rule that was triggered. ExMo uses a new approach to extract decision rules from the training data using term frequency-inverse document frequency (TF-IDF) features. With TF-IDF, decision rules with feature values that are more relevant to each class are extracted. Hence, the decision rules obtained by ExMo can distinguish the positive and negative classes better than the decision rules used in the existing Bayesian Rule List (BRL) algorithm, obtained using the frequent pattern mining approach. The paper also shows that ExMo learns a qualitatively better model than BRL. Furthermore, ExMo demonstrates that the textual explanation can be provided in a human-friendly way so that the explanation can be easily understood by non-expert users. We validate ExMo on several datasets with different sizes to evaluate its efficacy. Experimental validation on a real-world fraud detection application shows that ExMo is 20% more accurate than BRL and that it achieves accuracy similar to those of deep learning models.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种计算决策规则的新方法，以构建更准确的可解释机器学习模型，表示为 ExMo。 ExMo 可解释的机器学习模型由一系列 IF...THEN... 语句组成，条件中包含决策规则。这样，ExMo 自然会使用触发的决策规则为预测提供解释。 ExMo 使用一种新方法，使用术语频率-逆文档频率 (TF-IDF) 特征从训练数据中提取决策规则。通过 TF-IDF，可以提取与每个类别更相关的特征值的决策规则。因此，ExMo 获得的决策规则可以比现有贝叶斯规则列表（BRL）算法中使用的决策规则（使用频繁模式挖掘方法获得）更好地区分正类和负类。该论文还表明，ExMo 学习的模型在质量上比 BRL 更好。此外，ExMo 还证明可以以人性化的方式提供文本解释，以便非专业用户也可以轻松理解该解释。我们在多个不同大小的数据集上验证 ExMo 以评估其功效。对现实世界欺诈检测应用程序的实验验证表明，ExMo 的准确率比 BRL 高 20%，并且其准确率与深度学习模型相似。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ExMo模型，利用TF-IDF特征生成可解释的决策规则，提升机器学习模型的准确性和可解释性。
AI.motivation: 现有可解释模型（如BRL）依赖频繁模式挖掘，难以有效捕捉特征相关性，导致解释性不足且准确率有限。
AI.method: 基于TF-IDF特征提取决策规则，通过逆频率权重筛选关键特征，构建IF...THEN...规则列表作为模型解释依据。
AI.result: 在多个数据集及欺诈检测场景中，ExMo准确率较BRL提升20%，且接近深度学习模型效果，同时生成人类可理解的文本解释。
AI.conclusion: ExMo通过TF-IDF特征优化决策规则生成，在保持高准确性的同时显著提升模型可解释性，适用于需要透明决策的场景。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出ExMo模型，利用TF-IDF特征生成可解释的决策规则，提升机器学习模型的准确性和可解释性。
ai_content.motivation: 现有可解释模型（如BRL）依赖频繁模式挖掘，难以有效捕捉特征相关性，导致解释性不足且准确率有限。

search_text: ExMo: Explainable AI Model using Inverse Frequency Decision Rules ExMo：使用逆频率决策规则的可解释人工智能模型 In this paper, we present a novel method to compute decision rules to build a more accurate interpretable machine learning model, denoted as ExMo. The ExMo interpretable machine learning model consists of a list of IF...THEN... statements with a decision rule in the condition. This way, ExMo naturally provides an explanation for a prediction using the decision rule that was triggered. ExMo uses a new approach to extract decision rules from the training data using term frequency-inverse document frequency (TF-IDF) features. With TF-IDF, decision rules with feature values that are more relevant to each class are extracted. Hence, the decision rules obtained by ExMo can distinguish the positive and negative classes better than the decision rules used in the existing Bayesian Rule List (BRL) algorithm, obtained using the frequent pattern mining approach. The paper also shows that ExMo learns a qualitatively better model than BRL. Furthermore, ExMo demonstrates that the textual explanation can be provided in a human-friendly way so that the explanation can be easily understood by non-expert users. We validate ExMo on several datasets with different sizes to evaluate its efficacy. Experimental validation on a real-world fraud detection application shows that ExMo is 20% more accurate than BRL and that it achieves accuracy similar to those of deep learning models. 在本文中，我们提出了一种计算决策规则的新方法，以构建更准确的可解释机器学习模型，表示为 ExMo。 ExMo 可解释的机器学习模型由一系列 IF...THEN... 语句组成，条件中包含决策规则。这样，ExMo 自然会使用触发的决策规则为预测提供解释。 ExMo 使用一种新方法，使用术语频率-逆文档频率 (TF-IDF) 特征从训练数据中提取决策规则。通过 TF-IDF，可以提取与每个类别更相关的特征值的决策规则。因此，ExMo 获得的决策规则可以比现有贝叶斯规则列表（BRL）算法中使用的决策规则（使用频繁模式挖掘方法获得）更好地区分正类和负类。该论文还表明，ExMo 学习的模型在质量上比 BRL 更好。此外，ExMo 还证明可以以人性化的方式提供文本解释，以便非专业用户也可以轻松理解该解释。我们在多个不同大小的数据集上验证 ExMo 以评估其功效。对现实世界欺诈检测应用程序的实验验证表明，ExMo 的准确率比 BRL 高 20%，并且其准确率与深度学习模型相似。 本文提出ExMo模型，利用TF-IDF特征生成可解释的决策规则，提升机器学习模型的准确性和可解释性。 Pradip Mainali,Ismini Psychoula,Fabien A. P. Petitcolas cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Dianhai Yu,Liang Shen,Hongxiang Hao,Weibao Gong,Huachao Wu,Jiang Bian,Lirong Dai,Haoyi Xiong
title: MoESys: A Distributed and Efficient Mixture-of-Experts Training and Inference System for Internet Services
title_zh: MoESys：用于互联网服务的分布式高效专家混合培训和推理系统
abstract: While modern internet services, such as chatbots, search engines, and online advertising, demand the use of large-scale deep neural networks (DNNs), distributed training and inference over heterogeneous computing systems are desired to facilitate these DNN models. Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is one the most common strategies to lower the cost of training subject to the overall size of models/data through gating and parallelism in a divide-and-conquer fashion. While DeepSpeed has made efforts in carrying out large-scale MoE training over heterogeneous infrastructures, the efficiency of training and inference could be further improved from several system aspects, including load balancing, communication/computation efficiency, and memory footprint limits. In this work, we present a novel MoESys that boosts efficiency in both large-scale training and inference. Specifically, in the training procedure, the proposed MoESys adopts an Elastic MoE training strategy with 2D prefetch and Fusion communication over Hierarchical storage, so as to enjoy efficient parallelisms. For scalable inference in a single node, especially when the model size is larger than GPU memory, MoESys builds the CPU-GPU memory jointly into a ring of sections to load the model, and executes the computation tasks across the memory sections in a round-robin manner for efficient inference. We carried out extensive experiments to evaluate MoESys, where MoESys successfully trains a Unified Feature Optimization (UFO) model with a Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts model of 12B parameters in 8 days on 48 A100 GPU cards. The comparison against the state-of-the-art shows that MoESys outperformed DeepSpeed with 33% higher throughput (tokens per second) in training and 13% higher throughput in inference in general. Particularly, under unbalanced MoE Tasks, e.g., UFO, MoESys achieved 64% higher throughput with 18% lower memory footprints.

In this work, we present a novel MoESys that boosts efficiency in both large-scale training and inference. Specifically, in the training procedure, the proposed MoESys adopts an Elastic MoE training strategy with 2D prefetch and Fusion communication over Hierarchical storage, so as to enjoy efficient parallelisms. For scalable inference in a single node, especially when the model size is larger than GPU memory, MoESys builds the CPU-GPU memory jointly into a ring of sections to load the model, and executes the computation tasks across the memory sections in a round-robin manner for efficient inference. We carried out extensive experiments to evaluate MoESys, where MoESys successfully trains a Unified Feature Optimization (UFO) model with a Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts model of 12B parameters in 8 days on 48 A100 GPU cards. The comparison against the state-of-the-art shows that MoESys outperformed DeepSpeed with 33% higher throughput (tokens per second) in training and 13% higher throughput in inference in general. Particularly, under unbalanced MoE Tasks, e.g., UFO, MoESys achieved 64% higher throughput with 18% lower memory footprints.
abstract_zh: 虽然聊天机器人、搜索引擎和在线广告等现代互联网服务需要使用大规模深度神经网络 (DNN)，但需要通过异构计算系统进行分布式训练和推理来促进这些 DNN 模型。专家混合 (MoE) 是最常见的策略之一，它通过分而治之的方式通过门控和并行性来降低模型/数据总体大小的训练成本。虽然DeepSpeed已经努力在异构基础设施上进行大规模MoE训练，但可以从负载平衡、通信/计算效率和内存占用限制等多个系统方面进一步提高训练和推理的效率。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的 MoESys，它可以提高大规模训练和推理的效率。具体来说，在训练过程中，所提出的MoESys采用了具有2D预取和分层存储上的融合通信的Elastic MoE训练策略，以便享受高效的并行性。为了实现单节点的可扩展推理，特别是当模型大小大于 GPU 内存时，MoESys 将 CPU-GPU 内存联合构建成一个环段来加载模型，并以循环方式跨内存段执行计算任务以实现高效推理。我们进行了广泛的实验来评估 MoESys，其中 MoESys 在 8 天内成功地在 48 个 A100 GPU 卡上使用 12B 参数的稀疏门控混合专家模型训练了统一特征优化 (UFO) 模型。与最先进技术的比较表明，MoESys 的性能优于 DeepSpeed，训练吞吐量（每秒令牌数）高出 33%，推理吞吐量高出 13%。特别是，在不平衡的 MoE 任务（例如 UFO）下，MoESys 的吞吐量提高了 64%，内存占用降低了 18%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为MoESys的分布式高效混合专家（MoE）训练和推理系统，旨在提升互联网服务中大规模深度神经网络（DNN）的训练与推理效率。
AI.motivation: 现代互联网服务需要大规模DNN模型，但现有系统在负载均衡、通信计算效率及内存限制方面仍有改进空间，需更高效的MoE训练和推理方案。
AI.method: 训练阶段采用弹性MoE策略结合2D预取和分层存储的融合通信；推理阶段通过CPU-GPU内存环形分段加载模型，并以轮询方式跨内存段执行计算任务。
AI.result: 在48块A100 GPU上8天内成功训练12B参数的UFO模型，训练吞吐量比DeepSpeed高33%，推理吞吐量高13%，且在不平衡任务下内存占用降低18%。
AI.conclusion: MoESys通过优化系统层面的通信、存储与计算，显著提升了MoE模型的训练和推理效率，适用于大规模互联网服务场景。

authors_text: Boxuan Chen,Ruifeng Zhang,Shaochu Wang,Liqiang Zhang,Yu Liu
title: Deep Learning-based Inertial Odometry for Pedestrian Tracking using Attention Mechanism and Res2Net Module
title_zh: 使用注意力机制和 Res2Net 模块的基于深度学习的惯性里程计进行行人跟踪
abstract: Pedestrian dead reckoning is a challenging task due to the low-cost inertial sensor error accumulation. Recent research has shown that deep learning methods can achieve impressive performance in handling this issue. In this letter, we propose inertial odometry using a deep learning-based velocity estimation method. The deep neural network based on Res2Net modules and two convolutional block attention modules is leveraged to restore the potential connection between the horizontal velocity vector and raw inertial data from a smartphone. Our network is trained using only fifty percent of the public inertial odometry dataset (RoNIN) data. Then, it is validated on the RoNIN testing dataset and another public inertial odometry dataset (OXIOD). Compared with the traditional step-length and heading system-based algorithm, our approach decreases the absolute translation error (ATE) by 76%-86%. In addition, compared with the state-of-the-art deep learning method (RoNIN), our method improves its ATE by 6%-31.4%.
summary: Pedestrian dead reckoning is a challenging task due to the low-cost inertial sensor error accumulation. Recent research has shown that deep learning methods can achieve impressive performance in handling this issue. In this letter, we propose inertial odometry using a deep learning-based velocity estimation method. The deep neural network based on Res2Net modules and two convolutional block attention modules is leveraged to restore the potential connection between the horizontal velocity vector and raw inertial data from a smartphone. Our network is trained using only fifty percent of the public inertial odometry dataset (RoNIN) data. Then, it is validated on the RoNIN testing dataset and another public inertial odometry dataset (OXIOD). Compared with the traditional step-length and heading system-based algorithm, our approach decreases the absolute translation error (ATE) by 76%-86%. In addition, compared with the state-of-the-art deep learning method (RoNIN), our method improves its ATE by 6%-31.4%.
abstract_zh: 由于低成本惯性传感器误差累积，行人航位推算是一项具有挑战性的任务。最近的研究表明，深度学习方法在处理这个问题上可以取得令人印象深刻的表现。在这封信中，我们提出了使用基于深度学习的速度估计方法的惯性里程计。利用基于 Res2Net 模块和两个卷积块注意力模块的深度神经网络来恢复水平速度矢量和来自智能手机的原始惯性数据之间的潜在联系。我们的网络仅使用 50% 的公共惯性里程计数据集 (RoNIN) 数据进行训练。然后，在 RoNIN 测试数据集和另一个公共惯性里程计数据集 (OXIOD) 上进行验证。与传统的基于步长和航向系统的算法相比，我们的方法将绝对平移误差（ATE）降低了76%-86%。此外，与最先进的深度学习方法（RoNIN）相比，我们的方法将其ATE提高了6%-31.4%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的惯性里程计方法，结合注意力机制与Res2Net模块，用于改善行人跟踪中的惯性传感器误差累积问题。
AI.motivation: 传统惯性导航因传感器误差累积导致行人跟踪精度下降，而现有深度学习方法对数据依赖性高且效果有限，需更高效的模型结构。
AI.method: 设计融合Res2Net模块与双卷积块注意力机制（CBAM）的深度神经网络，仅使用RoNIN数据集50%的数据训练，并在RoNIN与OXIOD数据集验证。
AI.result: 相较传统步长-方向算法，绝对平移误差（ATE）降低76%-86%；相比RoNIN方法，ATE提升6%-31.4%。
AI.conclusion: 所提模型通过注意力机制与Res2Net结构有效捕捉惯性数据特征，显著优于传统方法与现有深度学习方案，适用于高精度行人跟踪场景。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度学习的惯性里程计方法，结合注意力机制与Res2Net模块，用于改善行人跟踪中的惯性传感器误差累积问题。

search_text: Deep Learning-based Inertial Odometry for Pedestrian Tracking using Attention Mechanism and Res2Net Module 使用注意力机制和 Res2Net 模块的基于深度学习的惯性里程计进行行人跟踪 Pedestrian dead reckoning is a challenging task due to the low-cost inertial sensor error accumulation. Recent research has shown that deep learning methods can achieve impressive performance in handling this issue. In this letter, we propose inertial odometry using a deep learning-based velocity estimation method. The deep neural network based on Res2Net modules and two convolutional block attention modules is leveraged to restore the potential connection between the horizontal velocity vector and raw inertial data from a smartphone. Our network is trained using only fifty percent of the public inertial odometry dataset (RoNIN) data. Then, it is validated on the RoNIN testing dataset and another public inertial odometry dataset (OXIOD). Compared with the traditional step-length and heading system-based algorithm, our approach decreases the absolute translation error (ATE) by 76%-86%. In addition, compared with the state-of-the-art deep learning method (RoNIN), our method improves its ATE by 6%-31.4%. 由于低成本惯性传感器误差累积，行人航位推算是一项具有挑战性的任务。最近的研究表明，深度学习方法在处理这个问题上可以取得令人印象深刻的表现。在这封信中，我们提出了使用基于深度学习的速度估计方法的惯性里程计。利用基于 Res2Net 模块和两个卷积块注意力模块的深度神经网络来恢复水平速度矢量和来自智能手机的原始惯性数据之间的潜在联系。我们的网络仅使用 50% 的公共惯性里程计数据集 (RoNIN) 数据进行训练。然后，在 RoNIN 测试数据集和另一个公共惯性里程计数据集 (OXIOD) 上进行验证。与传统的基于步长和航向系统的算法相比，我们的方法将绝对平移误差（ATE）降低了76%-86%。此外，与最先进的深度学习方法（RoNIN）相比，我们的方法将其ATE提高了6%-31.4%。 本文提出一种基于深度学习的惯性里程计方法，结合注意力机制与Res2Net模块，用于改善行人跟踪中的惯性传感器误差累积问题。 Boxuan Chen,Ruifeng Zhang,Shaochu Wang,Liqiang Zhang,Yu Liu cs.RO

authors_text: Yousef A. Alohali,Yassin Abdelsamad,Tamer Mesallam,Fida Almuhawas,Abdulrahman Hagr,Mahmoud S. Fayed
title: Predicting electrode array impedance after one month from cochlear implantation surgery
title_zh: 预测人工耳蜗植入手术一个月后的电极阵列阻抗
abstract: Sensorineural hearing loss can be treated using Cochlear implantation. After this surgery using the electrode array impedance measurements, we can check the stability of the impedance value and the dynamic range. Deterioration of speech recognition scores could happen because of increased impedance values. Medicines used to do these measures many times during a year after the surgery. Predicting the electrode impedance could help in taking decisions to help the patient get better hearing. In this research we used a dataset of 80 patients of children who did cochlear implantation using MED-EL FLEX28 electrode array of 12 channels. We predicted the electrode impedance on each channel after 1 month from the surgery date. We used different machine learning algorithms like neural networks and decision trees. Our results indicates that the electrode impedance can be predicted, and the best algorithm is different based on the electrode channel. Also, the accuracy level varies between 66% and 100% based on the electrode channel when accepting an error range between 0 and 3 KO. Further research is required to predict the electrode impedance after three months, six months and one year.
summary: Sensorineural hearing loss can be treated using Cochlear implantation. After this surgery using the electrode array impedance measurements, we can check the stability of the impedance value and the dynamic range. Deterioration of speech recognition scores could happen because of increased impedance values. Medicines used to do these measures many times during a year after the surgery. Predicting the electrode impedance could help in taking decisions to help the patient get better hearing. In this research we used a dataset of 80 patients of children who did cochlear implantation using MED-EL FLEX28 electrode array of 12 channels. We predicted the electrode impedance on each channel after 1 month from the surgery date. We used different machine learning algorithms like neural networks and decision trees. Our results indicates that the electrode impedance can be predicted, and the best algorithm is different based on the electrode channel. Also, the accuracy level varies between 66% and 100% based on the electrode channel when accepting an error range between 0 and 3 KO. Further research is required to predict the electrode impedance after three months, six months and one year.
abstract_zh: 感音神经性听力损失可以通过人工耳蜗植入来治疗。本次手术后使用电极阵列阻抗测量，我们可以检查阻抗值的稳定性和动态范围。由于阻抗值增加，语音识别分数可能会下降。手术后一年内，药物曾多次执行这些措施。预测电极阻抗可以帮助做出决定，帮助患者获得更好的听力。在这项研究中，我们使用了 80 名儿童患者的数据集，他们使用 12 通道的 MED-EL FLEX28 电极阵列进行了人工耳蜗植入。我们预测了手术日期 1 个月后每个通道上的电极阻抗。我们使用不同的机器学习算法，例如神经网络和决策树。我们的结果表明电极阻抗是可以预测的，并且最佳算法根据电极通道而不同。此外，当接受 0 到 3 KO 之间的误差范围时，根据电极通道，准确度水平在 66% 到 100% 之间变化。需要进一步研究来预测三个月、六个月和一年后的电极阻抗。

AI.tldr: 该论文旨在利用机器学习算法预测耳蜗植入术后一个月电极阵列的阻抗值。
AI.motivation: 耳蜗植入术后电极阻抗的升高可能导致言语识别能力下降，预测阻抗有助于优化患者听力恢复方案。
AI.method: 基于80名儿童的MED-EL FLEX28电极阵列数据，采用神经网络和决策树等机器学习算法进行阻抗预测，误差范围设定为0-3kΩ。
AI.result: 不同电极通道的预测准确率在66%至100%之间波动，最佳算法因通道位置而异。
AI.conclusion: 电极阻抗可被有效预测，但需进一步研究术后三月、半年及一年的长期预测模型。

authors_text: Wonkeun Jo,Dongil Kim
title: Neural Additive Models for Nowcasting
title_zh: 用于临近预报的神经加性模型
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are one of the most highlighted methods in machine learning. However, as DNNs are black-box models, they lack explanatory power for their predictions. Recently, neural additive models (NAMs) have been proposed to provide this power while maintaining high prediction performance. In this paper, we propose a novel NAM approach for multivariate nowcasting (NC) problems, which comprise an important focus area of machine learning. For the multivariate time-series data used in NC problems, explanations should be considered for every input value to the variables at distinguishable time steps. By employing generalized additive models, the proposed NAM-NC successfully explains each input value's importance for multiple variables and time steps. Experimental results involving a toy example and two real-world datasets show that the NAM-NC predicts multivariate time-series data as accurately as state-of-the-art neural networks, while also providing the explanatory importance of each input value. We also examine parameter-sharing networks using NAM-NC to decrease their complexity, and NAM-MC's hard-tied feature net extracted explanations with good performance.
summary: Deep neural networks (DNNs) are one of the most highlighted methods in machine learning. However, as DNNs are black-box models, they lack explanatory power for their predictions. Recently, neural additive models (NAMs) have been proposed to provide this power while maintaining high prediction performance. In this paper, we propose a novel NAM approach for multivariate nowcasting (NC) problems, which comprise an important focus area of machine learning. For the multivariate time-series data used in NC problems, explanations should be considered for every input value to the variables at distinguishable time steps. By employing generalized additive models, the proposed NAM-NC successfully explains each input value's importance for multiple variables and time steps. Experimental results involving a toy example and two real-world datasets show that the NAM-NC predicts multivariate time-series data as accurately as state-of-the-art neural networks, while also providing the explanatory importance of each input value. We also examine parameter-sharing networks using NAM-NC to decrease their complexity, and NAM-MC's hard-tied feature net extracted explanations with good performance.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络（DNN）是机器学习中最受关注的方法之一。然而，由于 DNN 是黑盒模型，它们的预测缺乏解释力。最近，人们提出了神经加性模型（NAM）来提供这种能力，同时保持高预测性能。在本文中，我们针对多元临近预报（NC）问题提出了一种新颖的 NAM 方法，该方法构成了机器学习的一个重要焦点领域。对于 NC 问题中使用的多元时间序列数据，应考虑对可区分时间步长的变量的每个输入值进行解释。通过采用广义相加模型，所提出的 NAM-NC 成功解释了每个输入值对于多个变量和时间步长的重要性。涉及一个玩具示例和两个真实世界数据集的实验结果表明，NAM-NC 可以像最先进的神经网络一样准确地预测多元时间序列数据，同时还提供每个输入值的解释重要性。我们还使用 NAM-NC 来检查参数共享网络以降低其复杂性，并且 NAM-MC 的硬连接特征网络以良好的性能提取了解释。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于神经可加模型（NAM）的多变量nowcasting（NC）方法，旨在提升模型的可解释性同时保持预测性能。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络（DNN）虽性能优异，但作为黑箱模型缺乏可解释性；而现有NAM方法在多变量时间序列的nowcasting任务中尚未充分解决输入变量与时间步长的解释需求。
AI.method: 通过引入广义可加模型框架，构建NAM-NC模型以解释多变量时间序列中每个输入值的重要性；并采用参数共享网络降低模型复杂度，利用NAM-MC的硬绑定特征网络提取有效解释。
AI.result: 实验表明，NAM-NC在玩具示例和两个真实数据集上的预测精度与先进神经网络相当，同时能有效解释多变量多时间步长的输入重要性，参数共享策略进一步降低了模型复杂度。
AI.conclusion: NAM-NC在保持高预测精度的同时实现了多变量nowcasting任务的可解释性，参数共享机制为复杂模型的简化提供了可行方案。

search_text: Neural Additive Models for Nowcasting 用于临近预报的神经加性模型 Deep neural networks (DNNs) are one of the most highlighted methods in machine learning. However, as DNNs are black-box models, they lack explanatory power for their predictions. Recently, neural additive models (NAMs) have been proposed to provide this power while maintaining high prediction performance. In this paper, we propose a novel NAM approach for multivariate nowcasting (NC) problems, which comprise an important focus area of machine learning. For the multivariate time-series data used in NC problems, explanations should be considered for every input value to the variables at distinguishable time steps. By employing generalized additive models, the proposed NAM-NC successfully explains each input value's importance for multiple variables and time steps. Experimental results involving a toy example and two real-world datasets show that the NAM-NC predicts multivariate time-series data as accurately as state-of-the-art neural networks, while also providing the explanatory importance of each input value. We also examine parameter-sharing networks using NAM-NC to decrease their complexity, and NAM-MC's hard-tied feature net extracted explanations with good performance. 深度神经网络（DNN）是机器学习中最受关注的方法之一。然而，由于 DNN 是黑盒模型，它们的预测缺乏解释力。最近，人们提出了神经加性模型（NAM）来提供这种能力，同时保持高预测性能。在本文中，我们针对多元临近预报（NC）问题提出了一种新颖的 NAM 方法，该方法构成了机器学习的一个重要焦点领域。对于 NC 问题中使用的多元时间序列数据，应考虑对可区分时间步长的变量的每个输入值进行解释。通过采用广义相加模型，所提出的 NAM-NC 成功解释了每个输入值对于多个变量和时间步长的重要性。涉及一个玩具示例和两个真实世界数据集的实验结果表明，NAM-NC 可以像最先进的神经网络一样准确地预测多元时间序列数据，同时还提供每个输入值的解释重要性。我们还使用 NAM-NC 来检查参数共享网络以降低其复杂性，并且 NAM-MC 的硬连接特征网络以良好的性能提取了解释。 本文提出了一种基于神经可加模型（NAM）的多变量nowcasting（NC）方法，旨在提升模型的可解释性同时保持预测性能。 Wonkeun Jo,Dongil Kim cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Juhee Son,Jiho Jin,Haneul Yoo,JinYeong Bak,Kyunghyun Cho,Alice Oh
title: Translating Hanja Historical Documents to Contemporary Korean and English
title_zh: 将汉字历史文献翻译成当代韩语和英语
abstract: The Annals of Joseon Dynasty (AJD) contain the daily records of the Kings of Joseon, the 500-year kingdom preceding the modern nation of Korea. The Annals were originally written in an archaic Korean writing system, `Hanja', and were translated into Korean from 1968 to 1993. The resulting translation was however too literal and contained many archaic Korean words; thus, a new expert translation effort began in 2012. Since then, the records of only one king have been completed in a decade. In parallel, expert translators are working on English translation, also at a slow pace and produced only one king's records in English so far. Thus, we propose H2KE, a neural machine translation model, that translates historical documents in Hanja to more easily understandable Korean and to English. Built on top of multilingual neural machine translation, H2KE learns to translate a historical document written in Hanja, from both a full dataset of outdated Korean translation and a small dataset of more recently translated contemporary Korean and English. We compare our method against two baselines: a recent model that simultaneously learns to restore and translate Hanja historical document and a Transformer based model trained only on newly translated corpora. The experiments reveal that our method significantly outperforms the baselines in terms of BLEU scores for both contemporary Korean and English translations. We further conduct extensive human evaluation which shows that our translation is preferred over the original expert translations by both experts and non-expert Korean speakers.
summary: The Annals of Joseon Dynasty (AJD) contain the daily records of the Kings of Joseon, the 500-year kingdom preceding the modern nation of Korea. The Annals were originally written in an archaic Korean writing system, `Hanja', and were translated into Korean from 1968 to 1993. The resulting translation was however too literal and contained many archaic Korean words; thus, a new expert translation effort began in 2012. Since then, the records of only one king have been completed in a decade. In parallel, expert translators are working on English translation, also at a slow pace and produced only one king's records in English so far. Thus, we propose H2KE, a neural machine translation model, that translates historical documents in Hanja to more easily understandable Korean and to English. Built on top of multilingual neural machine translation, H2KE learns to translate a historical document written in Hanja, from both a full dataset of outdated Korean translation and a small dataset of more recently translated contemporary Korean and English. We compare our method against two baselines: a recent model that simultaneously learns to restore and translate Hanja historical document and a Transformer based model trained only on newly translated corpora. The experiments reveal that our method significantly outperforms the baselines in terms of BLEU scores for both contemporary Korean and English translations. We further conduct extensive human evaluation which shows that our translation is preferred over the original expert translations by both experts and non-expert Korean speakers.
abstract_zh: 《朝鲜王朝实录》(AJD) 包含朝鲜国王的日常记录，朝鲜是现代朝鲜国家之前的 500 年王国。 《年鉴》最初是用古老的韩语书写系统“汉字”写成的，并于 1968 年至 1993 年被翻译成韩语。然而，最终的翻译过于字面意思，包含许多古老的韩语单词；于是，新的专家翻译工作于2012年开始。自此，十年间只完成了一位国王的记录。与此同时，专家翻译人员也在进行英文翻译，但速度也很慢，到目前为止只翻译了一部国王的英文记录。因此，我们提出了 H2KE，一种神经机器翻译模型，它将汉字历史文档翻译成更容易理解的韩语和英语。 H2KE 建立在多语言神经机器翻译之上，学习从过时韩语翻译的完整数据集和最近翻译的当代韩语和英语的小数据集翻译用汉字编写的历史文档。我们将我们的方法与两个基线进行比较：一个同时学习恢复和翻译汉字历史文档的最新模型和一个仅在新翻译的语料库上训练的基于 Transformer 的模型。实验表明，我们的方法在当代韩语和英语翻译的 BLEU 分数方面显着优于基线。我们进一步进行了广泛的人工评估，结果表明我们的翻译优于专家和非韩语专家的原始专家翻译。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种神经机器翻译模型H2KE，用于将朝鲜王朝汉籍文献从古韩文翻译为现代韩语和英语。
AI.motivation: 现有汉籍翻译过于直译且包含大量古语，难以被现代读者理解，需开发更准确的翻译方法。
AI.method: 基于多语言神经机器翻译框架，结合大量旧版韩语译文与少量新版当代韩语/英语译文数据训练模型，并与两种基线方法对比。
AI.result: H2KE在BLEU评分上显著优于基线模型，且人类评估显示其翻译质量优于原专家译文。
AI.conclusion: H2KE有效提升了历史文献的翻译质量，为研究和传播朝鲜王朝史料提供了更易理解的版本。

authors_text: Guogang Liao,Xuejian Li,Ze Wang,Fan Yang,Muzhi Guan,Bingqi Zhu,Yongkang Wang,Xingxing Wang,Dong Wang
title: NMA: Neural Multi-slot Auctions with Externalities for Online Advertising
title_zh: NMA：具有外部性的在线广告神经多槽拍卖
abstract: Online advertising driven by auctions brings billions of dollars in revenue for social networking services and e-commerce platforms. GSP auctions, which are simple and easy to understand for advertisers, have almost become the benchmark for ad auction mechanisms in the industry. However, most GSP-based industrial practices assume that the user click only relies on the ad itself, which overlook the effect of external items, referred to as externalities. Recently, DNA has attempted to upgrade GSP with deep neural networks and models local externalities to some extent. However, it only considers set-level contexts from auctions and ignores the order and displayed position of ads, which is still suboptimal. Although VCG-based multi-slot auctions (e.g., VCG, WVCG) make it theoretically possible to model global externalities (e.g., the order and positions of ads and so on), they lack an efficient balance of both revenue and social welfare. In this paper, we propose novel auction mechanisms named Neural Multi-slot Auctions (NMA) to tackle the above-mentioned challenges. Specifically, we model the global externalities effectively with a context-aware list-wise prediction module to achieve better performance. We design a list-wise deep rank module to guarantee incentive compatibility in end-to-end learning. Furthermore, we propose an auxiliary loss for social welfare to effectively reduce the decline of social welfare while maximizing revenue. Experiment results on both offline large-scale datasets and online A/B tests demonstrate that NMA obtains higher revenue with balanced social welfare than other existing auction mechanisms (i.e., GSP, DNA, WVCG) in industrial practice, and we have successfully deployed NMA on Meituan food delivery platform.
summary: Online advertising driven by auctions brings billions of dollars in revenue for social networking services and e-commerce platforms. GSP auctions, which are simple and easy to understand for advertisers, have almost become the benchmark for ad auction mechanisms in the industry. However, most GSP-based industrial practices assume that the user click only relies on the ad itself, which overlook the effect of external items, referred to as externalities. Recently, DNA has attempted to upgrade GSP with deep neural networks and models local externalities to some extent. However, it only considers set-level contexts from auctions and ignores the order and displayed position of ads, which is still suboptimal. Although VCG-based multi-slot auctions (e.g., VCG, WVCG) make it theoretically possible to model global externalities (e.g., the order and positions of ads and so on), they lack an efficient balance of both revenue and social welfare. In this paper, we propose novel auction mechanisms named Neural Multi-slot Auctions (NMA) to tackle the above-mentioned challenges. Specifically, we model the global externalities effectively with a context-aware list-wise prediction module to achieve better performance. We design a list-wise deep rank module to guarantee incentive compatibility in end-to-end learning. Furthermore, we propose an auxiliary loss for social welfare to effectively reduce the decline of social welfare while maximizing revenue. Experiment results on both offline large-scale datasets and online A/B tests demonstrate that NMA obtains higher revenue with balanced social welfare than other existing auction mechanisms (i.e., GSP, DNA, WVCG) in industrial practice, and we have successfully deployed NMA on Meituan food delivery platform.
abstract_zh: 由拍卖驱动的在线广告为社交网络服务和电子商务平台带来了数十亿美元的收入。普惠制拍卖对于广告主来说简单易懂，几乎已经成为行业广告拍卖机制的标杆。然而，大多数基于普惠制的行业实践都假设用户点击仅依赖于广告本身，从而忽略了外部因素（称为外部性）的影响。最近，DNA尝试用深度神经网络升级GSP，并在一定程度上模拟局部外部性。然而，它只考虑拍卖中的设置级别上下文，而忽略广告的顺序和显示位置，这仍然不是最佳的。尽管基于 VCG 的多时段拍卖（例如 VCG、WVCG）在理论上可以对全球外部性（例如广告的顺序和位置等）进行建模，但它们缺乏收入和社会福利的有效平衡。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为神经多时隙拍卖（NMA）的新型拍卖机制来应对上述挑战。具体来说，我们使用上下文感知的列表预测模块有效地对全球外部性进行建模，以实现更好的性能。我们设计了一个列表式深度排名模块来保证端到端学习中的激励兼容性。此外，我们提出社会福利辅助损失，以有效减少社会福利的下降，同时实现收入最大化。线下大规模数据集和线上A/B测试的实验结果表明，NMA在工业实践中比现有的其他拍卖机制（即GSP、DNA、WVCG）获得了更高的收入和均衡的社会福利，并且我们已经在美团外卖平台上成功部署了NMA。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为NMA的神经多槽拍卖机制，通过有效建模全局外部性，在兼顾收入和社会福利的前提下优化在线广告拍卖。
AI.motivation: 现有拍卖机制（如GSP、DNA、VCG）存在忽略广告位顺序和位置的局部外部性、或无法平衡收入与社会福利的缺陷，需设计更优的多槽拍卖方案。
AI.method: 设计基于上下文感知的列表级预测模块建模全局外部性，结合列表级深度排序模块保证激励相容性，并引入社会福利辅助损失函数优化平衡。
AI.result: 在离线大规模数据集和在线A/B测试中，NMA相较GSP、DNA、WVCG等机制实现更高收入且社会福利下降更少，已成功部署于美团外卖平台。
AI.conclusion: NMA通过全局外部性建模和多目标优化，在工业实践中验证了其在广告拍卖场景中兼顾收入与社会福利的有效性。

Recently, DNA has attempted to upgrade GSP with deep neural networks and models local externalities to some extent. However, it only considers set-level contexts from auctions and ignores the order and displayed position of ads, which is still suboptimal. Although VCG-based multi-slot auctions (e.g., VCG, WVCG) make it theoretically possible to model global externalities (e.g., the order and positions of ads and so on), they lack an efficient balance of both revenue and social welfare. In this paper, we propose novel auction mechanisms named Neural Multi-slot Auctions (NMA) to tackle the above-mentioned challenges. Specifically, we model the global externalities effectively with a context-aware list-wise prediction module to achieve better performance. We design a list-wise deep rank module to guarantee incentive compatibility in end-to-end learning. Furthermore, we propose an auxiliary loss for social welfare to effectively reduce the decline of social welfare while maximizing revenue. Experiment results on both offline large-scale datasets and online A/B tests demonstrate that NMA obtains higher revenue with balanced social welfare than other existing auction mechanisms (i.e., GSP, DNA, WVCG) in industrial practice, and we have successfully deployed NMA on Meituan food delivery platform. 由拍卖驱动的在线广告为社交网络服务和电子商务平台带来了数十亿美元的收入。普惠制拍卖对于广告主来说简单易懂，几乎已经成为行业广告拍卖机制的标杆。然而，大多数基于普惠制的行业实践都假设用户点击仅依赖于广告本身，从而忽略了外部因素（称为外部性）的影响。最近，DNA尝试用深度神经网络升级GSP，并在一定程度上模拟局部外部性。然而，它只考虑拍卖中的设置级别上下文，而忽略广告的顺序和显示位置，这仍然不是最佳的。尽管基于 VCG 的多时段拍卖（例如 VCG、WVCG）在理论上可以对全球外部性（例如广告的顺序和位置等）进行建模，但它们缺乏收入和社会福利的有效平衡。在本文中，我们提出了一种名为神经多时隙拍卖（NMA）的新型拍卖机制来应对上述挑战。具体来说，我们使用上下文感知的列表预测模块有效地对全球外部性进行建模，以实现更好的性能。我们设计了一个列表式深度排名模块来保证端到端学习中的激励兼容性。此外，我们提出社会福利辅助损失，以有效减少社会福利的下降，同时实现收入最大化。线下大规模数据集和线上A/B测试的实验结果表明，NMA在工业实践中比现有的其他拍卖机制（即GSP、DNA、WVCG）获得了更高的收入和均衡的社会福利，并且我们已经在美团外卖平台上成功部署了NMA。 本文提出一种名为NMA的神经多槽拍卖机制，通过有效建模全局外部性，在兼顾收入和社会福利的前提下优化在线广告拍卖。 Guogang Liao,Xuejian Li,Ze Wang,Fan Yang,Muzhi Guan,Bingqi Zhu,Yongkang Wang,Xingxing Wang,Dong Wang cs.AI

authors_text: Wenshuai Zhao,Zhiyuan Li,Joni Pajarinen
title: Learning Progress Driven Multi-Agent Curriculum
title_zh: 学习进步驱动的多智能体课程
abstract: The number of agents can be an effective curriculum variable for controlling the difficulty of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) tasks. Existing work typically uses manually defined curricula such as linear schemes. We identify two potential flaws while applying existing reward-based automatic curriculum learning methods in MARL: (1) The expected episode return used to measure task difficulty has high variance; (2) Credit assignment difficulty can be exacerbated in tasks where increasing the number of agents yields higher returns which is common in many MARL tasks. To address these issues, we propose to control the curriculum by using a TD-error based *learning progress* measure and by letting the curriculum proceed from an initial context distribution to the final task specific one. Since our approach maintains a distribution over the number of agents and measures learning progress rather than absolute performance, which often increases with the number of agents, we alleviate problem (2). Moreover, the learning progress measure naturally alleviates problem (1) by aggregating returns. In three challenging sparse-reward MARL benchmarks, our approach outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
summary: The number of agents can be an effective curriculum variable for controlling the difficulty of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) tasks. Existing work typically uses manually defined curricula such as linear schemes. We identify two potential flaws while applying existing reward-based automatic curriculum learning methods in MARL: (1) The expected episode return used to measure task difficulty has high variance; (2) Credit assignment difficulty can be exacerbated in tasks where increasing the number of agents yields higher returns which is common in many MARL tasks. To address these issues, we propose to control the curriculum by using a TD-error based *learning progress* measure and by letting the curriculum proceed from an initial context distribution to the final task specific one. Since our approach maintains a distribution over the number of agents and measures learning progress rather than absolute performance, which often increases with the number of agents, we alleviate problem (2). Moreover, the learning progress measure naturally alleviates problem (1) by aggregating returns. In three challenging sparse-reward MARL benchmarks, our approach outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
abstract_zh: 智能体的数量可以成为控制多智能体强化学习（MARL）任务难度的有效课程变量。现有的工作通常使用手动定义的课程，例如线性方案。我们在 MARL 中应用现有的基于奖励的自动课程学习方法时发现了两个潜在缺陷：（1）用于衡量任务难度的预期情节回报具有较高的方差； (2) 在增加智能体数量会产生更高回报的任务中，信用分配难度可能会加剧，这在许多 MARL 任务中很常见。为了解决这些问题，我们建议通过使用基于 TD 错误的“学习进度”度量来控制课程，并让课程从最初的上下文分布进行到最终的特定任务分布。由于我们的方法维持代理数量的分布并衡量学习进度而不是绝对性能（绝对性能通常随着代理数量的增加而增加），因此我们缓解了问题（2）。此外，学习进度衡量标准通过聚合回报自然地缓解了问题（1）。在三个具有挑战性的稀疏奖励 MARL 基准中，我们的方法优于最先进的基准。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于学习进度的多智能体课程学习方法，通过解决现有方法中任务难度衡量方差大和信用分配困难的问题，提升多智能体强化学习效果。
AI.motivation: 现有MARL课程学习方法依赖手动定义的课程或奖励驱动的自动方法，但存在任务难度衡量方差高、增加智能体数量导致信用分配困难等缺陷。
AI.method: 采用TD误差计算的学习进度指标替代传统回报衡量，并通过从初始上下文分布到任务特定分布的渐进式课程设计，结合智能体数量分布控制来缓解问题。
AI.result: 在三个稀疏奖励的MARL基准测试中，该方法性能优于当前最先进的基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 基于学习进度的课程学习框架有效解决了MARL中的难度控制与信用分配难题，为复杂多智能体任务提供了新的训练思路。

authors_text: Bingzhe Wu,Jintang Li,Junchi Yu,Yatao Bian,Hengtong Zhang,CHaochao Chen,Chengbin Hou,Guoji Fu,Liang Chen,Tingyang Xu,Yu Rong,Xiaolin Zheng,Junzhou Huang,Ran He,Baoyuan Wu,GUangyu Sun,Peng Cui,Zibin Zheng,Zhe Liu,Peilin Zhao
title: A Survey of Trustworthy Graph Learning: Reliability, Explainability, and Privacy Protection
title_zh: 可信图学习调查：可靠性、可解释性和隐私保护
comment: Preprint; Work in progress. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.07114
comments: Preprint; Work in progress. arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2202.07114
abstract: Deep graph learning has achieved remarkable progresses in both business and scientific areas ranging from finance and e-commerce, to drug and advanced material discovery. Despite these progresses, how to ensure various deep graph learning algorithms behave in a socially responsible manner and meet regulatory compliance requirements becomes an emerging problem, especially in risk-sensitive domains. Trustworthy graph learning (TwGL) aims to solve the above problems from a technical viewpoint. In contrast to conventional graph learning research which mainly cares about model performance, TwGL considers various reliability and safety aspects of the graph learning framework including but not limited to robustness, explainability, and privacy. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive review of recent leading approaches in the TwGL field from three dimensions, namely, reliability, explainability, and privacy protection. We give a general categorization for existing work and review typical work for each category. To give further insights for TwGL research, we provide a unified view to inspect previous works and build the connection between them. We also point out some important open problems remaining to be solved in the future developments of TwGL.
summary: Deep graph learning has achieved remarkable progresses in both business and scientific areas ranging from finance and e-commerce, to drug and advanced material discovery. Despite these progresses, how to ensure various deep graph learning algorithms behave in a socially responsible manner and meet regulatory compliance requirements becomes an emerging problem, especially in risk-sensitive domains. Trustworthy graph learning (TwGL) aims to solve the above problems from a technical viewpoint. In contrast to conventional graph learning research which mainly cares about model performance, TwGL considers various reliability and safety aspects of the graph learning framework including but not limited to robustness, explainability, and privacy. In this survey, we provide a comprehensive review of recent leading approaches in the TwGL field from three dimensions, namely, reliability, explainability, and privacy protection. We give a general categorization for existing work and review typical work for each category. To give further insights for TwGL research, we provide a unified view to inspect previous works and build the connection between them. We also point out some important open problems remaining to be solved in the future developments of TwGL.
abstract_zh: 深度图学习在金融和电子商务、药物和先进材料发现等商业和科学领域取得了显着进展。尽管取得了这些进展，但如何确保各种深度图学习算法以对社会负责的方式运行并满足监管合规要求成为一个新出现的问题，特别是在风险敏感领域。可信图学习（TwGL）旨在从技术角度解决上述问题。与主要关心模型性能的传统图学习研究相比，TwGL 考虑了图学习框架的各种可靠性和安全性，包括但不限于鲁棒性、可解释性和隐私性。在本次调查中，我们从可靠性、可解释性和隐私保护三个维度对 TwGL 领域最新的领先方法进行了全面回顾。我们对现有工作进行一般分类，并回顾每个类别的典型工作。为了给 TwGL 研究提供进一步的见解，我们提供了一个统一的视图来检查以前的工作并在它们之间建立联系。我们还指出了 TwGL 未来发展中仍有待解决的一些重要的开放性问题。

AI.tldr: 综述可信图学习领域的最新方法，涵盖可靠性、可解释性和隐私保护三个维度
AI.motivation: 尽管深度图学习在多个领域取得进展，但如何确保其社会负责性和符合法规要求成为关键问题，特别是在风险敏感领域
AI.method: 通过分类现有工作，回顾各类别典型研究，并建立联系，提供统一视角分析前人工作
AI.result: 系统梳理TwGL领域研究，明确分类并指出未来研究方向
AI.conclusion: 强调TwGL研究的重要性，并指出未来需解决的关键挑战

authors_text: Abhijit Suprem,Sanjyot Vaidya,Suma Cherkadi,Purva Singh,Joao Eduardo Ferreira,Calton Pu
title: Constructive Interpretability with CoLabel: Corroborative Integration, Complementary Features, and Collaborative Learning
title_zh: CoLabel 的建设性可解释性：佐证整合、互补特征和协作学习
abstract: Machine learning models with explainable predictions are increasingly sought after, especially for real-world, mission-critical applications that require bias detection and risk mitigation. Inherent interpretability, where a model is designed from the ground-up for interpretability, provides intuitive insights and transparent explanations on model prediction and performance. In this paper, we present CoLabel, an approach to build interpretable models with explanations rooted in the ground truth. We demonstrate CoLabel in a vehicle feature extraction application in the context of vehicle make-model recognition (VMMR). CoLabel performs VMMR with a composite of interpretable features such as vehicle color, type, and make, all based on interpretable annotations of the ground truth labels. First, CoLabel performs corroborative integration to join multiple datasets that each have a subset of desired annotations of color, type, and make. Then, CoLabel uses decomposable branches to extract complementary features corresponding to desired annotations. Finally, CoLabel fuses them together for final predictions. During feature fusion, CoLabel harmonizes complementary branches so that VMMR features are compatible with each other and can be projected to the same semantic space for classification. With inherent interpretability, CoLabel achieves superior performance to the state-of-the-art black-box models, with accuracy of 0.98, 0.95, and 0.94 on CompCars, Cars196, and BoxCars116K, respectively. CoLabel provides intuitive explanations due to constructive interpretability, and subsequently achieves high accuracy and usability in mission-critical situations.
summary: Machine learning models with explainable predictions are increasingly sought after, especially for real-world, mission-critical applications that require bias detection and risk mitigation. Inherent interpretability, where a model is designed from the ground-up for interpretability, provides intuitive insights and transparent explanations on model prediction and performance. In this paper, we present CoLabel, an approach to build interpretable models with explanations rooted in the ground truth. We demonstrate CoLabel in a vehicle feature extraction application in the context of vehicle make-model recognition (VMMR). CoLabel performs VMMR with a composite of interpretable features such as vehicle color, type, and make, all based on interpretable annotations of the ground truth labels. First, CoLabel performs corroborative integration to join multiple datasets that each have a subset of desired annotations of color, type, and make. Then, CoLabel uses decomposable branches to extract complementary features corresponding to desired annotations. Finally, CoLabel fuses them together for final predictions. During feature fusion, CoLabel harmonizes complementary branches so that VMMR features are compatible with each other and can be projected to the same semantic space for classification. With inherent interpretability, CoLabel achieves superior performance to the state-of-the-art black-box models, with accuracy of 0.98, 0.95, and 0.94 on CompCars, Cars196, and BoxCars116K, respectively. CoLabel provides intuitive explanations due to constructive interpretability, and subsequently achieves high accuracy and usability in mission-critical situations.
abstract_zh: 具有可解释预测的机器学习模型越来越受到追捧，特别是对于现实世界中需要偏差检测和风险缓解的关键任务应用程序。固有的可解释性，即从头开始设计模型以实现可解释性，为模型预测和性能提供直观的见解和透明的解释。在本文中，我们提出了 CoLabel，一种构建可解释模型的方法，其解释植根于事实真相。我们在车辆型号识别 (VMMR) 背景下的车辆特征提取应用中演示了 CoLabel。 CoLabel 使用车辆颜色、类型和制造商等可解释特征的组合来执行 VMMR，所有这些都基于地面实况标签的可解释注释。首先，CoLabel 执行协同集成来连接多个数据集，每个数据集都有所需的颜色、类型和品牌注释的子集。然后，CoLabel 使用可分解分支来提取与所需注释相对应的互补特征。最后，CoLabel 将它们融合在一起以进行最终预测。在特征融合过程中，CoLabel 协调互补分支，使得 VMMR 特征彼此兼容，并且可以投影到相同的语义空间进行分类。凭借固有的可解释性，CoLabel 实现了优于最先进黑盒模型的性能，在 CompCars、Cars196 和 BoxCars116K 上的准确度分别为 0.98、0.95 和 0.94。 CoLabel 由于具有建设性的可解释性而提供直观的解释，并随后在关键任务情况下实现高精度和可用性。

AI.motivation: 现实世界中关键任务需要可解释的机器学习模型以检测偏差和降低风险，传统黑盒模型缺乏透明性，需设计从底层保证可解释性的方法。

AI.conclusion: CoLabel通过构造性可解释性方法，在关键任务场景中实现了高准确性与直观解释性，为可解释模型设计提供了有效框架。

search_text: Constructive Interpretability with CoLabel: Corroborative Integration, Complementary Features, and Collaborative Learning CoLabel 的建设性可解释性：佐证整合、互补特征和协作学习 Machine learning models with explainable predictions are increasingly sought after, especially for real-world, mission-critical applications that require bias detection and risk mitigation. Inherent interpretability, where a model is designed from the ground-up for interpretability, provides intuitive insights and transparent explanations on model prediction and performance. In this paper, we present CoLabel, an approach to build interpretable models with explanations rooted in the ground truth. We demonstrate CoLabel in a vehicle feature extraction application in the context of vehicle make-model recognition (VMMR). CoLabel performs VMMR with a composite of interpretable features such as vehicle color, type, and make, all based on interpretable annotations of the ground truth labels. First, CoLabel performs corroborative integration to join multiple datasets that each have a subset of desired annotations of color, type, and make. Then, CoLabel uses decomposable branches to extract complementary features corresponding to desired annotations. Finally, CoLabel fuses them together for final predictions. During feature fusion, CoLabel harmonizes complementary branches so that VMMR features are compatible with each other and can be projected to the same semantic space for classification. With inherent interpretability, CoLabel achieves superior performance to the state-of-the-art black-box models, with accuracy of 0.98, 0.95, and 0.94 on CompCars, Cars196, and BoxCars116K, respectively. CoLabel provides intuitive explanations due to constructive interpretability, and subsequently achieves high accuracy and usability in mission-critical situations. 具有可解释预测的机器学习模型越来越受到追捧，特别是对于现实世界中需要偏差检测和风险缓解的关键任务应用程序。固有的可解释性，即从头开始设计模型以实现可解释性，为模型预测和性能提供直观的见解和透明的解释。在本文中，我们提出了 CoLabel，一种构建可解释模型的方法，其解释植根于事实真相。我们在车辆型号识别 (VMMR) 背景下的车辆特征提取应用中演示了 CoLabel。 CoLabel 使用车辆颜色、类型和制造商等可解释特征的组合来执行 VMMR，所有这些都基于地面实况标签的可解释注释。首先，CoLabel 执行协同集成来连接多个数据集，每个数据集都有所需的颜色、类型和品牌注释的子集。然后，CoLabel 使用可分解分支来提取与所需注释相对应的互补特征。最后，CoLabel 将它们融合在一起以进行最终预测。在特征融合过程中，CoLabel 协调互补分支，使得 VMMR 特征彼此兼容，并且可以投影到相同的语义空间进行分类。凭借固有的可解释性，CoLabel 实现了优于最先进黑盒模型的性能，在 CompCars、Cars196 和 BoxCars116K 上的准确度分别为 0.98、0.95 和 0.94。 CoLabel 由于具有建设性的可解释性而提供直观的解释，并随后在关键任务情况下实现高精度和可用性。 本文提出CoLabel方法，通过可解释的特征融合实现车辆型号识别（VMMR）任务，结合地面真实标注构建可解释模型。 Abhijit Suprem,Sanjyot Vaidya,Suma Cherkadi,Purva Singh,Joao Eduardo Ferreira,Calton Pu cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Nagita Mehrseresht
title: Action parsing using context features
title_zh: 使用上下文特征进行动作解析
comment: ef:International Conference on Digital Image Computing: Techniques and Applications (DICTA), 2017, pp. 1-7
comments: ef:International Conference on Digital Image Computing: Techniques and Applications (DICTA), 2017, pp. 1-7
abstract: We propose an action parsing algorithm to parse a video sequence containing an unknown number of actions into its action segments. We argue that context information, particularly the temporal information about other actions in the video sequence, is valuable for action segmentation. The proposed parsing algorithm temporally segments the video sequence into action segments. The optimal temporal segmentation is found using a dynamic programming search algorithm that optimizes the overall classification confidence score. The classification score of each segment is determined using local features calculated from that segment as well as context features calculated from other candidate action segments of the sequence. Experimental results on the Breakfast activity data-set showed improved segmentation accuracy compared to existing state-of-the-art parsing techniques.
summary: We propose an action parsing algorithm to parse a video sequence containing an unknown number of actions into its action segments. We argue that context information, particularly the temporal information about other actions in the video sequence, is valuable for action segmentation. The proposed parsing algorithm temporally segments the video sequence into action segments. The optimal temporal segmentation is found using a dynamic programming search algorithm that optimizes the overall classification confidence score. The classification score of each segment is determined using local features calculated from that segment as well as context features calculated from other candidate action segments of the sequence. Experimental results on the Breakfast activity data-set showed improved segmentation accuracy compared to existing state-of-the-art parsing techniques.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种动作解析算法，将包含未知数量动作的视频序列解析为其动作片段。我们认为上下文信息，特别是视频序列中其他动作的时间信息，对于动作分割很有价值。所提出的解析算法将视频序列暂时分割成动作片段。使用动态规划搜索算法找到最佳时间分割，该算法优化整体分类置信度得分。每个片段的分类分数是使用从该片段计算的局部特征以及从序列的其他候选动作片段计算的上下文特征来确定的。早餐活动数据集的实验结果表明，与现有最先进的解析技术相比，分割精度有所提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用上下文特征的动作解析算法，将包含未知数量动作的视频序列分割为动作片段。
AI.motivation: 作者认为视频中其他动作的时序上下文信息对动作分割任务具有重要价值，现有方法未充分挖掘此类信息。
AI.method: 采用动态规划搜索算法优化分割方案，结合当前片段的局部特征与其他候选片段的上下文特征计算分类置信度。
AI.result: 在Breakfast数据集的实验中，该方法的分割准确率优于现有最先进的解析技术。
AI.conclusion: 上下文特征的有效利用能显著提升动作解析性能，验证了时序信息对动作分割的关键作用。

authors_text: Geonho Cha,Ho-Deok Jang,Dongyoon Wee
title: Self-Supervised Depth Estimation with Isometric-Self-Sample-Based Learning
title_zh: 基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计
abstract: Managing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation has been a main issue for handling the self-supervised depth estimation problem. Most previous methods have alleviated this issue by removing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation based on the masks estimated from another module, making it difficult to fully utilize the training images. In this paper, to handle this problem, we propose an isometric self-sample-based learning (ISSL) method to fully utilize the training images in a simple yet effective way. The proposed method provides additional supervision during training using self-generated images that comply with pure static scene assumption. Specifically, the isometric self-sample generator synthesizes self-samples for each training image by applying random rigid transformations on the estimated depth. Thus both the generated self-samples and the corresponding training image always follow the static scene assumption. We show that plugging our ISSL module into several existing models consistently improves the performance by a large margin. In addition, it also boosts the depth accuracy over different types of scene, i.e., outdoor scenes (KITTI and Make3D) and indoor scene (NYUv2), validating its high effectiveness.
summary: Managing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation has been a main issue for handling the self-supervised depth estimation problem. Most previous methods have alleviated this issue by removing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation based on the masks estimated from another module, making it difficult to fully utilize the training images. In this paper, to handle this problem, we propose an isometric self-sample-based learning (ISSL) method to fully utilize the training images in a simple yet effective way. The proposed method provides additional supervision during training using self-generated images that comply with pure static scene assumption. Specifically, the isometric self-sample generator synthesizes self-samples for each training image by applying random rigid transformations on the estimated depth. Thus both the generated self-samples and the corresponding training image always follow the static scene assumption. We show that plugging our ISSL module into several existing models consistently improves the performance by a large margin. In addition, it also boosts the depth accuracy over different types of scene, i.e., outdoor scenes (KITTI and Make3D) and indoor scene (NYUv2), validating its high effectiveness.
abstract_zh: 管理光度损失公式中的动态区域一直是处理自监督深度估计问题的主要问题。以前的大多数方法都通过根据另一个模块估计的掩模去除光度损失公式中的动态区域来缓解这个问题，从而难以充分利用训练图像。在本文中，为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于等距自样本学习（ISSL）的方法，以简单而有效的方式充分利用训练图像。所提出的方法在训练期间使用符合纯静态场景假设的自生成图像提供额外的监督。具体来说，等距自样本生成器通过对估计深度应用随机刚性变换来合成每个训练图像的自样本。因此，生成的自样本和相应的训练图像始终遵循静态场景假设。我们证明，将我们的 ISSL 模块插入到几个现有模型中可以持续大幅提高性能。此外，它还提高了不同类型场景的深度精度，即室外场景（KITTI和Make3D）和室内场景（NYUv2），验证了其高效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计方法（ISSL），通过合成符合静态场景假设的自样本来提升深度估计性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法通过移除动态区域来处理光度损失中的动态问题，但这种方式限制了训练图像的充分利用，难以实现更优的深度估计。
AI.method: 设计等距自样本生成器，对估计的深度图应用随机刚性变换生成自样本，确保生成的样本与原图均满足静态场景假设，从而提供额外的训练监督。
AI.result: 将ISSL模块集成到多种现有模型中，在KITTI、Make3D（室外场景）和NYUv2（室内场景）数据集上均显著提升了深度估计精度。
AI.conclusion: ISSL方法通过有效利用训练图像中的静态信息，在不同场景下均表现出优异的性能，为自监督深度估计提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计方法（ISSL），通过合成符合静态场景假设的自样本来提升深度估计性能。

ai_content_json.conclusion: ISSL方法通过有效利用训练图像中的静态信息，在不同场景下均表现出优异的性能，为自监督深度估计提供了新思路。

tldr: 本文提出了一种基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计方法（ISSL），通过合成符合静态场景假设的自样本来提升深度估计性能。
motivation: 现有方法通过移除动态区域来处理光度损失中的动态问题，但这种方式限制了训练图像的充分利用，难以实现更优的深度估计。
method: 设计等距自样本生成器，对估计的深度图应用随机刚性变换生成自样本，确保生成的样本与原图均满足静态场景假设，从而提供额外的训练监督。
result: 将ISSL模块集成到多种现有模型中，在KITTI、Make3D（室外场景）和NYUv2（室内场景）数据集上均显著提升了深度估计精度。
conclusion: ISSL方法通过有效利用训练图像中的静态信息，在不同场景下均表现出优异的性能，为自监督深度估计提供了新思路。
search_text: Self-Supervised Depth Estimation with Isometric-Self-Sample-Based Learning 基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计 Managing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation has been a main issue for handling the self-supervised depth estimation problem. Most previous methods have alleviated this issue by removing the dynamic regions in the photometric loss formulation based on the masks estimated from another module, making it difficult to fully utilize the training images. In this paper, to handle this problem, we propose an isometric self-sample-based learning (ISSL) method to fully utilize the training images in a simple yet effective way. The proposed method provides additional supervision during training using self-generated images that comply with pure static scene assumption. Specifically, the isometric self-sample generator synthesizes self-samples for each training image by applying random rigid transformations on the estimated depth. Thus both the generated self-samples and the corresponding training image always follow the static scene assumption. We show that plugging our ISSL module into several existing models consistently improves the performance by a large margin. In addition, it also boosts the depth accuracy over different types of scene, i.e., outdoor scenes (KITTI and Make3D) and indoor scene (NYUv2), validating its high effectiveness. 管理光度损失公式中的动态区域一直是处理自监督深度估计问题的主要问题。以前的大多数方法都通过根据另一个模块估计的掩模去除光度损失公式中的动态区域来缓解这个问题，从而难以充分利用训练图像。在本文中，为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种基于等距自样本学习（ISSL）的方法，以简单而有效的方式充分利用训练图像。所提出的方法在训练期间使用符合纯静态场景假设的自生成图像提供额外的监督。具体来说，等距自样本生成器通过对估计深度应用随机刚性变换来合成每个训练图像的自样本。因此，生成的自样本和相应的训练图像始终遵循静态场景假设。我们证明，将我们的 ISSL 模块插入到几个现有模型中可以持续大幅提高性能。此外，它还提高了不同类型场景的深度精度，即室外场景（KITTI和Make3D）和室内场景（NYUv2），验证了其高效性。 本文提出了一种基于等距自样本学习的自监督深度估计方法（ISSL），通过合成符合静态场景假设的自样本来提升深度估计性能。 Geonho Cha,Ho-Deok Jang,Dongyoon Wee cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Ioannis Sarridis,Christos Koutlis,Giorgos Kordopatis-Zilos,Ioannis Kompatsiaris,Symeon Papadopoulos
title: InDistill: Information flow-preserving knowledge distillation for model compression
title_zh: InDistill：用于模型压缩的信息流保留知识蒸馏
abstract: In this paper, we introduce InDistill, a method that serves as a warmup stage for enhancing Knowledge Distillation (KD) effectiveness. InDistill focuses on transferring critical information flow paths from a heavyweight teacher to a lightweight student. This is achieved via a training scheme based on curriculum learning that considers the distillation difficulty of each layer and the critical learning periods when the information flow paths are established. This procedure can lead to a student model that is better prepared to learn from the teacher. To ensure the applicability of InDistill across a wide range of teacher-student pairs, we also incorporate a pruning operation when there is a discrepancy in the width of the teacher and student layers. This pruning operation reduces the width of the teacher's intermediate layers to match those of the student, allowing direct distillation without the need for an encoding stage. The proposed method is extensively evaluated using various pairs of teacher-student architectures on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet datasets demonstrating that preserving the information flow paths consistently increases the performance of the baseline KD approaches on both classification and retrieval settings. The code is available at https://github.com/gsarridis/InDistill.
summary: In this paper, we introduce InDistill, a method that serves as a warmup stage for enhancing Knowledge Distillation (KD) effectiveness. InDistill focuses on transferring critical information flow paths from a heavyweight teacher to a lightweight student. This is achieved via a training scheme based on curriculum learning that considers the distillation difficulty of each layer and the critical learning periods when the information flow paths are established. This procedure can lead to a student model that is better prepared to learn from the teacher. To ensure the applicability of InDistill across a wide range of teacher-student pairs, we also incorporate a pruning operation when there is a discrepancy in the width of the teacher and student layers. This pruning operation reduces the width of the teacher's intermediate layers to match those of the student, allowing direct distillation without the need for an encoding stage. The proposed method is extensively evaluated using various pairs of teacher-student architectures on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet datasets demonstrating that preserving the information flow paths consistently increases the performance of the baseline KD approaches on both classification and retrieval settings. The code is available at https://github.com/gsarridis/InDistill.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了 InDistill，这是一种作为增强知识蒸馏（KD）有效性的预热阶段的方法。 InDistill 专注于将关键信息流路径从重量级教师转移到轻量级学生。这是通过基于课程学习的培训方案来实现的，该方案考虑了各层的蒸馏难度以及信息流路径建立时的关键学习时段。此过程可以产生一个学生模型，为向老师学习做好更好的准备。为了确保 InDistill 在广泛的师生对中的适用性，当教师和学生层的宽度存在差异时，我们还结合了剪枝操作。这种修剪操作减少了教师中间层的宽度以匹配学生的中间层，从而允许直接蒸馏而无需编码阶段。使用 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 数据集上的各种师生架构对所提出的方法进行了广泛评估，证明保留信息流路径始终可以提高基线 KD 方法在分类和检索设置上的性能。该代码可从 https://github.com/gsarridis/InDistill 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出InDistill方法，通过保留信息流路径提升知识蒸馏效果，实现模型压缩。
AI.motivation: 传统知识蒸馏方法未能有效传递教师模型中的关键信息流路径，导致学生模型性能受限。
AI.method: 基于课程学习的训练方案，结合层间难度评估与关键学习阶段的信息流保留，并通过剪枝操作适配教师-学生层宽差异。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100和ImageNet数据集上，InDistill显著提升基线知识蒸馏方法在分类和检索任务中的性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过信息流路径保护的预训练阶段可有效增强知识蒸馏效果，为模型压缩提供新思路。
